> that's independent of whether business models and broader economy is going to explode.

It's independent of the former, but not the later. That is my point.

Businesses and business models fail all the time, does not mean the 'broader economy is going to explode'.

It could, sure. But that has been predicted several hundred times and happened only a few times.

blocks.html_en:
Economies of scale.

You need a cluster of 8-12 H100s to run the largest models locally.

It doesn't make sense to run these locally yet unless your use case also involves making it available for several dozen concurrent users.

sentences.en: Economies of scale.You need a cluster of 8-12 H100s to run the largest models locally.It doesn't make sense to run these locally yet unless your use case also involves making it available for several dozen concurrent users.
sentences.zh: 规模经济。您需要一个由 8-12 个 H100 组成的集群才能在本地运行最大的模型。除非您的用例还涉及使其可供数十个并发用户使用，否则在本地运行这些模型没有意义。

blocks.html_en:
I think this fails to take into account how many people are fine with "fast enough" vs "fastest".

I've seen people happily use AI that takes several minutes to generate text or edit an image because to them they already aren't using their computer when they tell it to start; they just grab their phone and walk away and come back only to check in on it.

I feel like people here and on other technology discussions -- although it's worse here -- don't seem to parse what being the minority means.

They know they're one of the few to have access to such incredible hardware -- whether it be rented or purchased for way too much cash -- but they only see their own kin; their own ilk. They only compare themselves to the best.

The reality is that nobody expects data centre speed nor power in their own home and are satisfied to just go "haha its thinking" and let their computer quietly tick in the background as opposed to paying outragious prices for subscriptions or hardware.

sentences.en: I think this fails to take into account how many people are fine with "fast enough" vs "fastest".I've seen people happily use AI that takes several minutes to generate text or edit an image because to them they already aren't using their computer when they tell it to start; they just grab their phone and walk away and come back only to check in on it.I feel like people here and on other technology discussions -- although it's worse here -- don't seem to parse what being the minority means.They know they're one of the few to have access to such incredible hardware -- whether it be rented or purchased for way too much cash -- but they only see their own kin; their own ilk.
sentences.zh: 我认为这没有考虑到有多少人对“足够快”和“最快”感到满意。我看到人们很高兴地使用人工智能，需要几分钟的时间来生成文本或编辑图像，因为对他们来说，当他们告诉计算机启动时，他们已经没有在使用计算机了;他们只是抓起手机走开，然后回来查看一下。我觉得这里的人和其他技术讨论中的人们——尽管这里更糟糕——似乎并没有解析少数派意味着什么。他们知道自己是少数能够使用如此令人难以置信的硬件的人之一——无论是租用还是花费太多现金购买——但他们只看到自己的亲属；他们自己的同类。

sentences.en: They only compare themselves to the best.The reality is that nobody expects data centre speed nor power in their own home and are satisfied to just go "haha its thinking" and let their computer quietly tick in the background as opposed to paying outragious prices for subscriptions or hardware.
sentences.zh: 他们只将自己与最好的进行比较。现实情况是，没有人期望自己家中的数据中心速度或功率，并且满足于只是“哈哈它的想法”，让他们的计算机在后台安静地运行，而不是为订阅或硬件支付离谱的价格。

blocks.html_en:
Unless you're a developer or doing complicated research you do not need much to use an LLM at home with consumer-grade hardware.

I'm editing photos with 32GB of DDR4 RAM at 3200mhz alongside a 3050 with a nearly decade-old mobo and ryzen 5800XT and at most? It takes 30 seconds and that's allowing the gpu to use my actual RAM as 'fallback' memory.

There are photoshop filters -now- that take longer than that before the LLM craze even began. Hell; I can train a lora in an hour or two.

This obsession with "more specs more data faster and faster" isn't going to win; it already isn't winning.

Deal with it or get wrecked.

sentences.en: Unless you're a developer or doing complicated research you do not need much to use an LLM at home with consumer-grade hardware.I'm editing photos with 32GB of DDR4 RAM at 3200mhz alongside a 3050 with a nearly decade-old mobo and ryzen 5800XT and at most?
sentences.zh: 除非你是一名开发人员或进行复杂的研究，否则你不需要太多在家里使用消费级硬件的法学硕士。我正在使用 32GB DDR4 RAM、3200mhz 编辑照片，旁边是 3050、近十年前的主板和 ryzen 5800XT，最多？

sentences.en: It takes 30 seconds and that's allowing the gpu to use my actual RAM as 'fallback' memory.There are photoshop filters -now- that take longer than that before the LLM craze even began.
sentences.zh: 它需要 30 秒，这允许 GPU 使用我的实际 RAM 作为“后备”内存。现在有 Photoshop 滤镜，比 LLM 热潮开始之前需要的时间更长。

sentences.en: Hell; I can train a lora in an hour or two.This obsession with "more specs more data faster and faster" isn't going to win; it already isn't winning.Deal with it or get wrecked.
sentences.zh: 地狱;我可以在一两个小时内训练一个劳拉。 这种对“更多规格、更多数据、越来越快”的痴迷是不会成功的；它已经没有获胜。要么处理它，要么被毁。

blocks.html_en: The per token costs plummet with more concurrents. A box that can do 100 tps at request depth 1 might be able to do 3000 tps at request depth 64. Less per thread, but massively more per GPU/joule/etc. That’s the economy of scale of running in a DC rather than locally that they were referring to.

sentences.en: The per token costs plummet with more concurrents.
sentences.zh: 随着并发数的增加，每个代币的成本急剧下降。

sentences.en: A box that can do 100 tps at request depth 1 might be able to do 3000 tps at request depth 64.
sentences.zh: 在请求深度 1 时可以执行 100 tps 的盒子可能能够在请求深度 64 时执行 3000 tps。

sentences.en: Less per thread, but massively more per GPU/joule/etc. That’s the economy of scale of running in a DC rather than locally that they were referring to.
sentences.zh: 每个线程更少，但每个 GPU/焦耳/等更多。他们指的是在特区而非本地运行的规模经济。

blocks.html_en:
It's not the exact same bet, but it rhymes.

It worked out for Uber, they are wildly profitable now after spending a decade losing money.

Also worked out as Amazon managed to outlast all the dotcom era e-commerce competitors while being unprofitable.

sentences.en: It's not the exact same bet, but it rhymes.It worked out for Uber, they are wildly profitable now after spending a decade losing money.Also worked out as Amazon managed to outlast all the dotcom era e-commerce competitors while being unprofitable.
sentences.zh: 这不是完全相同的赌注，但押韵。这对 Uber 来说是成功的，在经历了十年亏损之后，他们现在已经实现了巨额盈利。亚马逊在不盈利的情况下设法超越了所有互联网时代的电子商务竞争对手，也取得了成功。

> It worked out for Uber, they are wildly profitable now after spending a decade losing money.

blocks.html_en:
Uber is a two sided marketplace and was still famously unprofitable for at the same time.

Over 14 years, Uber burnt ~31 Billion dollars and in nearly whereas the amount invested within AI seems to be within Trillions at this point and in near future with a product which doesn't have much moat and shaky financials on profit.

sentences.zh: Uber 是一个双向市场，并且在同一时间仍然没有盈利。14 年来，Uber 烧掉了约 310 亿美元，而在人工智能领域的投资额目前和不久的将来似乎都在数万亿美元之内，而该产品没有太多护城河，而且财务状况也不稳定。

Does that include the capital costs of spinning up to the current models/scale or is it just running costs?

Also, lost revenue from other services being degraded by shifting resources to supporting training/serving models (Google Search...)?

blocks.html_en:
> They think can outlast their competitors and capture a larger portion of the pie while the cost of inference keeps going down dramatically.

We are also within an arms race of training newer larger models with more speed while discontinuing older models.

Gemini/Chatgpt have already discontinued their models from 2024 (iirc) because they are using all their compute in serving/training newer models. Being quite frank, nobody is serving a model from 2024 as the intended use-case while having very little moat as open source models are catching up.

> Of course there will be market crashes and corrections and things like that and most companies won't survive, but the bet is that whoever survives ends up doing pretty well.

How so, by raising the prices? because the current prices aren't sustainable and I feel as if there would certainly be companies which will try for one reason or other to be cheaper to capture the market share because of the larger promise of whoever is able to get as market share. I had once thought about it and I don't think that even in an ideal world, they would end up doing pretty well given no moat.

Also even if a company survives and ends up being one of the survivors and makes profit in the ideal scenario you mention, then within some years other companies will try again and construct more datacenters and end up driving the prices down for everyone, so nobody knows how things might look down for 2-3 years let alone a decade, so I remain a bit skeptic currently so.

I had actually thought some on the economics of datacenters and I found it to be very related to power. The only ones which seems to be making money might be the power generators actually because power is the actual bottleneck rather than GPU's in datacenters from my understanding.

Though the power is raised at the cost of electricity bill increases for everybody including people living in houses. The job prospects are minimal as well, as a nation, aside from just getting investment just for the sake of it because AI's trendy right now, I feel like its a net negative deal for people living there.

sentences.en: > They think can outlast their competitors and capture a larger portion of the pie while the cost of inference keeps going down dramatically.We are also within an arms race of training newer larger models with more speed while discontinuing older models.Gemini/Chatgpt have already discontinued their models from 2024 (iirc) because they are using all their compute in serving/training newer models.
sentences.zh: > 他们认为可以超越竞争对手并占领更大份额，同时推理成本不断大幅下降。我们还处于一场军备竞赛，以更快的速度训练更新的更大模型，同时停止使用旧模型。Gemini/Chatgpt 已经从 2024 年 (iirc) 开始停止使用他们的模型，因为他们将所有计算都用于服务/训练新模型。

sentences.en: Being quite frank, nobody is serving a model from 2024 as the intended use-case while having very little moat as open source models are catching up.> Of course there will be market crashes and corrections and things like that and most companies won't survive, but the bet is that whoever survives ends up doing pretty well.How so, by raising the prices?
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，没有人将 2024 年的模型作为预期用例，而随着开源模型的追赶，几乎没有什么护城河。>当然，会有市场崩溃和调整以及类似的事情，大多数公司将无法生存，但赌注是，无论谁生存下来，最终都会做得很好。那么，通过提高价格呢？

sentences.en: because the current prices aren't sustainable and I feel as if there would certainly be companies which will try for one reason or other to be cheaper to capture the market share because of the larger promise of whoever is able to get as market share.
sentences.zh: 因为目前的价格是不可持续的，而且我觉得肯定会有一些公司会出于某种原因尝试以更便宜的价格来占领市场份额，因为谁能够获得市场份额，谁就有更大的承诺。

sentences.en: I had once thought about it and I don't think that even in an ideal world, they would end up doing pretty well given no moat.Also even if a company survives and ends up being one of the survivors and makes profit in the ideal scenario you mention, then within some years other companies will try again and construct more datacenters and end up driving the prices down for everyone, so nobody knows how things might look down for 2-3 years let alone a decade, so I remain a bit skeptic currently so.I had actually thought some on the economics of datacenters and I found it to be very related to power.
sentences.zh: 我曾经想过这个问题，我不认为即使在理想的世界中，如果没有护城河，他们最终也会做得很好。而且，即使一家公司生存下来，最终成为幸存者之一，并在你提到的理想情况下盈利，那么在几年之内，其他公司将再次尝试并建造更多的数据中心，最终压低每个人的价格，所以没有人知道情况会在 2-3 年内下降，更不用说十年了，所以我目前仍然有点怀疑。一些关于数据中心经济学的内容，我发现它与电力非常相关。

sentences.en: The only ones which seems to be making money might be the power generators actually because power is the actual bottleneck rather than GPU's in datacenters from my understanding.Though the power is raised at the cost of electricity bill increases for everybody including people living in houses.
sentences.zh: 唯一看起来赚钱的可能是发电机，因为根据我的理解，电力才是真正的瓶颈，而不是数据中心的 GPU。尽管电力的提高是以每个人（包括住在房子里的人）的电费增加为代价的。

sentences.en: The job prospects are minimal as well, as a nation, aside from just getting investment just for the sake of it because AI's trendy right now, I feel like its a net negative deal for people living there.
sentences.zh: 作为一个国家，就业前景也很渺茫，除了因为人工智能现在很流行而仅仅为了获得投资之外，我觉得这对生活在那里的人们来说是一个净负面交易。

blocks.html_en:
> Data centers are NOT real estate. Buildings and power lines usually last 30-50 years. GPUs become obsolete in 5 years.

Data centers are real estate. One of the big players in carrier neutral data centers even calls themselves Digitial Realty.

The contents of the DC is not real estate. But neither is the an office or a house or a warehouse.

sentences.en: But neither is the an office or a house or a warehouse.
sentences.zh: 但它既不是办公室，也不是房子，也不是仓库。

blocks.html_en:
Right but their payback period is insanely short - a $5M GB200 NVL72 cluster is expected to generate $75M in revenue over 3 years for inference providers. That's a 3 month payback period.

AND - they're operating well past their estimated service lifetime.

blocks.html_en: I would be more careful before assuming 5 years depreciation schedule. Currently price tags are attached to computing power, not the production cost. Computing is not getting meaningfully cheaper with newer GPUs but it only allows better scaling, which makes older hardware more relevant for many use cases. This is why A100 is still selling like hotcakes. I don't think this trend will change soon.

sentences.en: I would be more careful before assuming 5 years depreciation schedule.
sentences.zh: 在假设 5 年折旧计划之前我会更加小心。

sentences.en: Currently price tags are attached to computing power, not the production cost.
sentences.zh: 目前价格标签与计算能力挂钩，而不是生产成本。

sentences.en: Computing is not getting meaningfully cheaper with newer GPUs but it only allows better scaling, which makes older hardware more relevant for many use cases.
sentences.zh: 更新的 GPU 并没有让计算成本显着降低，但它只允许更好的扩展，这使得旧硬件与许多用例更相关。

sentences.en: This is why A100 is still selling like hotcakes.
sentences.zh: 这也是A100依然热销的原因。

sentences.en: I don't think this trend will change soon.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种趋势不会很快改变。

blocks.html_en: Apple can enjoy because it controls a significant fraction of consumer computing platform so they can simply collect tax from everyone else. This is not true for the rest of big tech. Only Google has Android but it cannot sit and enjoy because they still don't control hardware and AI is an existential problem for their search business.

sentences.en: Apple can enjoy because it controls a significant fraction of consumer computing platform so they can simply collect tax from everyone else.
sentences.zh: 苹果可以享受这一好处，因为它控制着消费者计算平台的很大一部分，因此他们可以简单地向其他人征税。

sentences.en: This is not true for the rest of big tech.
sentences.zh: 对于其他大型科技公司来说，情况并非如此。

sentences.en: Only Google has Android but it cannot sit and enjoy because they still don't control hardware and AI is an existential problem for their search business.
sentences.zh: 只有谷歌拥有Android，但它不能坐下来享受，因为他们仍然无法控制硬件，而人工智能对于他们的搜索业务来说是一个生存问题。

blocks.html_en:
> GPUs become obsolete in 5 years

The GPUs are far from worthless after 5 years. E.g. the A100 80GB PCIe version cost around $15k when it was introduced in 2021 and now sells for $10k used.

Things might be slightly worse for the data center servers, but I am sure they will find find buyers.

sentences.en: > GPUs become obsolete in 5 yearsThe GPUs are far from worthless after 5 years.
sentences.zh: > GPU 5 年后就会过时5 年后 GPU 远非毫无价值。

sentences.en: E.g.
sentences.zh: 例如。

sentences.en: the A100 80GB PCIe version cost around $15k when it was introduced in 2021 and now sells for $10k used.Things might be slightly worse for the data center servers, but I am sure they will find find buyers.
sentences.zh: A100 80GB PCIe 版本于 2021 年推出时售价约为 1.5 万美元，现在二手售价为 1 万美元。数据中心服务器的情况可能会稍微糟糕一些，但我相信他们会找到买家。

blocks.html_en: They only reason that they are retaining value is there was not so much demand for GPUs in 2021 as there is today. Once the demand drops you will find then in dumpsters across our barren, burning dystopia.

sentences.en: They only reason that they are retaining value is there was not so much demand for GPUs in 2021 as there is today.
sentences.zh: 他们之所以能够保留价值，唯一的理由是 2021 年对 GPU 的需求不会像今天那么大。

sentences.en: Once the demand drops you will find then in dumpsters across our barren, burning dystopia.
sentences.zh: 一旦需求下降，你就会在我们贫瘠、燃烧的反乌托邦的垃圾箱里找到它。

blocks.html_en: They hold value as there is insane demand. The same reason a consumer RTX4090 costs more today than bew in 2021. Once the tide drops enough for hardware lead times to shorten to weeks, they will go the way of other used DC hardware - written off after 5 years.

sentences.en: They hold value as there is insane demand.
sentences.zh: 由于存在疯狂的需求，它们具有价值。

sentences.en: The same reason a consumer RTX4090 costs more today than bew in 2021.
sentences.zh: 与 2021 年消费类 RTX4090 的成本比现在更高的原因相同。

sentences.en: Once the tide drops enough for hardware lead times to shorten to weeks, they will go the way of other used DC hardware - written off after 5 years.
sentences.zh: 一旦潮流下降到足以使硬件交付时间缩短至几周，它们将重蹈其他二手 DC 硬件的覆辙——5 年后报废。

blocks.html_en:
For what its worth, SK Hynix CEO has every incentive to show that RAM prices will remain high for as long as possible because that is the only thing which is floating their evaluation to such astronomical amounts.

Independent estimates sort of show around 2027-28 from what I remember.

I remember reading some article which said that RAM prices are already going down from its peak slowly (IIRC I can be wrong, I usually am but 3-5% month from its absolute peak) but the current RAM prices are still astronomical given past rates but the RAM prices will slow down hopefully sooner rather than later.

sentences.en: For what its worth, SK Hynix CEO has every incentive to show that RAM prices will remain high for as long as possible because that is the only thing which is floating their evaluation to such astronomical amounts.Independent estimates sort of show around 2027-28 from what I remember.I remember reading some article which said that RAM prices are already going down from its peak slowly (IIRC I can be wrong, I usually am but 3-5% month from its absolute peak) but the current RAM prices are still astronomical given past rates but the RAM prices will slow down hopefully sooner rather than later.
sentences.zh: 就其价值而言，SK Hynix 首席执行官完全有动力表明 RAM 价格将尽可能长时间地保持在高位，因为这是唯一使他们的评估浮动到如此天文数字的因素。根据我的记忆，独立估计显示大约在 2027-28 年左右。我记得读过一篇文章，说 RAM 价格已经从峰值缓慢下降（IIRC 我可能是错的，我通常距离绝对峰值只有 3-5%），但当前 RAM 价格仍然是考虑到过去的价格，这是一个天文数字，但 RAM 价格有望尽快放缓。

blocks.html_en:
It's not directly relevant, but the company has been involved in multiple criminal investigations with alleged price fixing etc.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DRAM_price_fixing_scandal

Also, I don't recall any era where "proof" is required for allegations. "Evidence" is probably prudent, but proof is usually produced when the accused gets ends up in court.

Unless you time travelled from a feudal era where they throw you in jail for slandering the nobleman or something.

blocks.html_en:
> Back in the day, we used to have this thing called “proof” before making wild allegations of illegality

I understand that you might've wanted to know more sources but its better to ask them directly in good faith as I am more than happy to provide sources and have a good faith discussion! :)

> are you just playing pretend?

(This part feels a bit personally rude to me. I would prefer if you could omit this part of the messages in future or take care of that, Hackernews is a lovely community for the most part so lets keep it that way :-D)

I appreciated reading the comment of @hnfong. The DRAM Price fixing scandal and multiple criminal investigations are something that I didn't even know of!

The general consensus is that its late 27-28 and I would like to share some of the sources that you were asking.

I don't know if they are lying or not but the interview on reuters was conducted on the same day that they went on Nasdaq

> "We forecast that next year will be the worst year in the industry's history from the supply perspective," Kwak told Reuters in an interview on Friday, the day the South Korean memory chipmaker began trading on Nasdaq.[From (1)]

> Multiple analysts push meaningful relief to late 2027 at earliest — with some estimates stretching into 2028[0]

> UBS likewise expects the global DRAM industry to remain undersupplied until at least the second quarter of 2028.[1]

The above one [1] reference is literally from the reuter article itself which was referenced in the parent comment

> Across the U.S., China and Europe, DDR5 RAM prices fell sharply. For example, after reaching an all-time high of $500, Corsair’s Vengeance 32GB kit is now down to $369 on Amazon. Memory has fallen more than 20% from recent peak prices, and stock prices of major memory factories like Micron, Samsung and SK Hynix have seen drops of over 20%.[2]

[0]: https://iqondigital.com/learn/pc-news/when-will-ram-prices-d...

[1]: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/sk-hynix-ceo-sees...

[2]: https://www.tomsguide.com/computing/hardware/ram-prices-are-...

have a nice day with the sources and let me know if you want a good faith discussion!

sentences.zh: DRAM价格操纵丑闻和多项刑事调查是我什至不知道的事情！普遍的共识是27-28月底，我想分享你所询问的一些消息来源。我不知道他们是否在撒谎，但路透社的采访是在他们登上纳斯达克的同一天进行的>“我们预测，从供应角度来看，明年将是该行业历史上最糟糕的一年，”郭在周五接受路透社采访时告诉路透社，这家韩国内存芯片制造商开始在纳斯达克交易的那一天。[来自 (1)]> 多名分析师将有意义的缓解最早推迟到 2027 年末，一些估计会持续到 2028 年[0]> 瑞银同样预计全球 DRAM 行业至少在 2028 年第二季度之前仍将供应不足。[1]上述 [1] 引用实际上来自路透社文章本身，该文章在母评论中引用>在美国各地，中国和欧洲，DDR5 RAM价格大幅下跌。

blocks.html_en: > Across the U.S., China and Europe, DDR5 RAM prices fell sharply. For example, after reaching an all-time high of $500, Corsair’s Vengeance 32GB kit is now down to $369 on Amazon. blocks.html_en:
> GPUs become obsolete in 5 years.

Not only that, but they're typically amortized over 5 years, where the actual lifespan usually falls far shorter (1-3 years), adding to the artificial subsidy conditions we see today. So they're gaming the lenders into deferring interest payments as much as possible today so that new competitors don't have the same cheap financing advantage.[0]

0: https://blog.citp.princeton.edu/2025/10/15/lifespan-of-ai-ch...

blocks.html_en:
If they're deliberately inflating the likely useful economic life of their assets to get a lower interest rate, it's hard to see how that wouldn't be classed as fraud.

It's the sort of behaviour that really does end up with people going to prison.

sentences.en: If they're deliberately inflating the likely useful economic life of their assets to get a lower interest rate, it's hard to see how that wouldn't be classed as fraud.It's the sort of behaviour that really does end up with people going to prison.
sentences.zh: 如果他们故意夸大资产的可用经济寿命以获得较低的利率，那么很难看出这怎么能不被归类为欺诈。这种行为确实最终会导致人们入狱。

blocks.html_en:
You’re all getting some concepts mixed up here. That five year amortization rate is the IRS’ usual amortization rate for computers. GPUs are classed as computers for asset depreciation purposes. But GPUs are part of 168(k) so they’re eligible for a 100% bonus depreciation the year of purchase.

There’s nothing fraudulent at all here just people using terms they really aren’t comfortable with.

sentences.en: You’re all getting some concepts mixed up here.
sentences.zh: 你们都混淆了一些概念。

sentences.en: That five year amortization rate is the IRS’ usual amortization rate for computers.
sentences.zh: 该五年摊销率是美国国税局通常的计算机摊销率。

sentences.en: GPUs are classed as computers for asset depreciation purposes.
sentences.zh: GPU 被归类为用于资产折旧目的的计算机。

sentences.en: But GPUs are part of 168(k) so they’re eligible for a 100% bonus depreciation the year of purchase.There’s nothing fraudulent at all here just people using terms they really aren’t comfortable with.
sentences.zh: 但 GPU 属于 168(k) 的一部分，因此它们有资格在购买当年享受 100% 的额外折旧。这里根本没有任何欺诈行为，只是人们使用了他们真正不舒服的术语。

That sounds reasonable, it's "just" $1k/yr for 2B workers (there are about 1.2B total "knowledge workers" in the world including gig drivers), or $10k/yr for 200M workers (there are 70M office and technical workers in the US). /s

https://www.dpeaflcio.org/factsheets/the-professional-and-te...

In 4 years it better be 10x more important to have than a cell phone is today, or 10x more important than having internet/monitor/pc/printer is for an office worker today.

It's super-intelligence or bust.

sentences.en: That sounds reasonable, it's "just" $1k/yr for 2B workers (there are about 1.2B total "knowledge workers" in the world including gig drivers), or $10k/yr for 200M workers (there are 70M office and technical workers in the US). /shttps://www.dpeaflcio.org/factsheets/the-professional-and-te...In 4 years it better be 10x more important to have than a cell phone is today, or 10x more important than having internet/monitor/pc/printer is for an office worker today.It's super-intelligence or bust.
sentences.zh: 这听起来很合理，对于 2B 名工人（世界上大约有 1.2B 名“知识工人”，包括零工司机）来说“只需”1000 美元/年，或者对于 2 亿工人（美国有 7000 万办公室和技术工人）来说每年 1 万美元。 /shttps://www.dpeaflcio.org/factsheets/the-professional-and-te...在 4 年内，对于办公室工作人员来说，拥有手机的重要性可能比现在的手机重要 10 倍，或者比拥有互联网/显示器/电脑/打印机的重要性还要重要 10 倍。这是超级智能，否则就会破产。

blocks.html_en: The math looks good on paper, but in reality enterprise AI is hard, most companies are realizing they are actually not seeing ROI from AI. One of my customers took about 8 months to rollout an AI initiative that by the time it launched and people got trained on it, it was already legacy. Also if you are 10x more productive with AI that doesn’t necessarily increases your billable output. There could be some super models like Mythos aimed at very specific hard tasks like drug development, but we have not seen any of that yet, and the clock is ticking.

sentences.en: The math looks good on paper, but in reality enterprise AI is hard, most companies are realizing they are actually not seeing ROI from AI.
sentences.zh: 纸面上的数学看起来不错，但实际上企业人工智能很难，大多数公司意识到他们实际上没有看到人工智能的投资回报率。

sentences.en: One of my customers took about 8 months to rollout an AI initiative that by the time it launched and people got trained on it, it was already legacy.
sentences.zh: 我的一位客户花了大约 8 个月的时间推出了一项人工智能计划，当它启动并且人们接受培训时，它已经成为遗产。

sentences.en: Also if you are 10x more productive with AI that doesn’t necessarily increases your billable output.
sentences.zh: 此外，如果人工智能使您的工作效率提高 10 倍，这并不一定会增加您的计费产出。

sentences.en: There could be some super models like Mythos aimed at very specific hard tasks like drug development, but we have not seen any of that yet, and the clock is ticking.
sentences.zh: 可能会有一些像 Mythos 这样的超级模型，旨在完成药物开发等非常具体的艰巨任务，但我们还没有看到任何这样的东西，而且时间正在流逝。

blocks.html_en: Yes, I'm agreeing with you. There need to be 200 companies willing to pay $10B/yr for this. What is the ROI? That's the pay roll of ~half the work force of the largest 200 companies. Unless you can fire %50 your employees, everything else is a sunk cost you already own.

sentences.en: Yes, I'm agreeing with you.
sentences.zh: 是的，我同意你的观点。

sentences.en: There need to be 200 companies willing to pay $10B/yr for this.
sentences.zh: 需要有 200 家公司愿意每年为此支付 10B 美元。

sentences.en: What is the ROI?
sentences.zh: 投资回报率是多少？

sentences.en: That's the pay roll of ~half the work force of the largest 200 companies.
sentences.zh: 这相当于最大 200 家公司大约一半员工的工资。

sentences.en: Unless you can fire %50 your employees, everything else is a sunk cost you already own.
sentences.zh: 除非你能解雇 50% 的员工，否则其他一切都是你已经拥有的沉没成本。

blocks.html_en: I just don’t understand this view. This is the most significant technology ever developed. The uncertainty currently is whether it 1) has massive impact, completely altering society and the making world significantly significantly better or 2) if we go into a fast takeoff/rsi loop. Personally I’ve always been highly skeptical of the later, but that seems like a genuine possibility now. It’s not ‘are we going to be able to generate 10% roic on compute’ the answer to that is yes.

sentences.en: I just don’t understand this view.
sentences.zh: 我只是不明白这个观点。

sentences.en: This is the most significant technology ever developed.
sentences.zh: 这是有史以来最重要的技术。

sentences.en: The uncertainty currently is whether it 1) has massive impact, completely altering society and the making world significantly significantly better or 2) if we go into a fast takeoff/rsi loop.
sentences.zh: 目前的不确定性在于它是否 1) 产生巨大影响，彻底改变社会并使世界显着变得更好，或者 2) 如果我们进入快速起飞/RSI 循环。

sentences.en: Personally I’ve always been highly skeptical of the later, but that seems like a genuine possibility now.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我一直对后者持高度怀疑态度，但现在看来确实有可能。

sentences.en: It’s not ‘are we going to be able to generate 10% roic on compute’ the answer to that is yes.
sentences.zh: 这并不是“我们是否能够通过计算产生 10% 的 roic”，答案是肯定的。

blocks.html_en: It will obviously be a large part of the economy like online shopping is today. The companies that built up a lot of debt to be brand names in online shopping primarily went bankrupt because new companies had no debt (and perhaps no negative sentiment from early customer experiences.)

sentences.en: It will obviously be a large part of the economy like online shopping is today.
sentences.zh: 显然，这将成为经济的很大一部分，就像今天的网上购物一样。

sentences.en: The companies that built up a lot of debt to be brand names in online shopping primarily went bankrupt because new companies had no debt (and perhaps no negative sentiment from early customer experiences.)
sentences.zh: 那些为了成为在线购物品牌而背负大量债务的公司主要破产了，因为新公司没有债务（也许也没有早期客户体验带来的负面情绪。）

blocks.html_en: What destroyed hardware manufacturers after the first bubble burst was all the old but overpowered server hardware ending up in resale after bankruptcies. Hard to get hardware is a bad investment that is also potentially obsolete after new optimizations make the next generation much more efficient. The companies that think their individual optimizations are going to outpace industry wide optimization are delusional, historically speaking.

sentences.en: What destroyed hardware manufacturers after the first bubble burst was all the old but overpowered server hardware ending up in resale after bankruptcies.
sentences.zh: 第一次泡沫破裂后，摧毁硬件制造商的是所有旧的但功能强大的服务器硬件，最终在破产后被转售。

sentences.en: Hard to get hardware is a bad investment that is also potentially obsolete after new optimizations make the next generation much more efficient.
sentences.zh: 很难获得硬件是一项糟糕的投资，在新的优化使下一代更加高效之后，它也可能会过时。

sentences.en: The companies that think their individual optimizations are going to outpace industry wide optimization are delusional, historically speaking.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，那些认为自己的个人优化将超过整个行业优化的公司是妄想。

blocks.html_en:
> the hyperscalars are acting like Amazon in 1998

I hope you remember furniture.com, pets.com, webvan.com, kozmo.com and many others.

Amazon.com (during 1998) in some sense is the exception, not the norm from the bloodbath in stock markets during the dot com bubble.

(I highly recommend the book How the internet happened for more knowledge about things before, during and after the dot com bubble.)

sentences.en: > the hyperscalars are acting like Amazon in 1998I hope you remember furniture.com, pets.com, webvan.com, kozmo.com and many others.Amazon.com (during 1998) in some sense is the exception, not the norm from the bloodbath in stock markets during the dot com bubble.(I highly recommend the book How the internet happened for more knowledge about things before, during and after the dot com bubble.)
sentences.zh: > 超标量的行为就像 1998 年的亚马逊一样。我希望您还记得 Furniture.com、pets.com、webvan.com、kozmo.com 和许多其他网站。Amazon.com（1998 年期间）在某种意义上是例外，而不是互联网泡沫期间股市大屠杀的常态。（我强烈推荐这本书《互联网是如何发生的》，以获取有关互联网泡沫之前、期间和之后的更多知识。）

blocks.html_en:
Looking at cash burn is looking at the wrong end of the horse. Some companies, like Meta, have burned huge piles of cash in pursuit of, for example, the Metaverse and they've got nothing to show for it, not even a slight increment in ad tech, and yet they earned enough to shrug it off.

There's a big difference between Google spending tens of billions on AI infrastructure and what Oracle is doing. Oracle is spending to get on a bandwagon. Google is transforming their business, so far seemingly correctly. If AI flops big-time, Google will be left with some stranded assets, but it won't be existential the way it would be to Oracle.

sentences.en: Looking at cash burn is looking at the wrong end of the horse.
sentences.zh: 仅仅关注现金消耗就已经找错了方向。

sentences.en: Some companies, like Meta, have burned huge piles of cash in pursuit of, for example, the Metaverse and they've got nothing to show for it, not even a slight increment in ad tech, and yet they earned enough to shrug it off.There's a big difference between Google spending tens of billions on AI infrastructure and what Oracle is doing.
sentences.zh: 一些公司，比如 Meta，为了追求 Metaverse 等投入了大量现金，但他们没有任何成果，甚至连广告技术都没有一点点增长，但他们赚到的钱却足以对此不屑一顾。谷歌在人工智能基础设施上花费数百亿美元与甲骨文正在做的事情之间存在很大差异。

sentences.en: Oracle is spending to get on a bandwagon.
sentences.zh: 甲骨文正在斥巨资追随潮流。

sentences.en: Google is transforming their business, so far seemingly correctly.
sentences.zh: 谷歌正在改变他们的业务，到目前为止似乎是正确的。

sentences.en: If AI flops big-time, Google will be left with some stranded assets, but it won't be existential the way it would be to Oracle.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能遭遇重大失败，谷歌将留下一些搁浅的资产，但它不会像甲骨文那样具有生存意义。

sentences.en: If AI flops big-time, Google will be left with some stranded assets, but it won't be existential the way it would be to Oracle.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能遭遇重大失败，谷歌将留下一些搁浅的资产，但它不会像甲骨文那样具有生存意义。

blocks.html_en: Looking at cash burn is looking at the wrong end of the horse. Some companies, like Meta, have burned huge piles of cash in pursuit of, for example, the Metaverse and they've got nothing to show for it, not even a slight increment in ad tech, and yet they earned enough to shrug it off.

sentences.en: Looking at cash burn is looking at the wrong end of the horse.

sentences.en: Some companies, like Meta, have burned huge piles of cash in pursuit of, for example, the Metaverse and they've got nothing to show for it, not even a slight increment in ad tech, and yet they earned enough to shrug it off.

blocks.html_en:
Smart glasses are about a 15 million unit per year business. Smart watches are about 150 million units per year. Meta owns about half the smart glasses business, but it hasn't provided them a platform they can control that has enough penetration to make a difference.

But wait it gets worse: since Meta hasn't got a platform comparable to a mainstream PC or mobile device OS, the Meta glasses business is vulnerable and subscale, as impressive as owning half the TAM is. Since Meta glasses aren't a companion to an existing platform (not even AndroidAR, even though they run Android) they will become a second tier choice as soon as Apple or Samsung ship a smart glasses product.

sentences.en: Smart glasses are about a 15 million unit per year business.
sentences.zh: 智能眼镜的年销量约为 1500 万副。

sentences.en: Smart watches are about 150 million units per year.
sentences.zh: 智能手表每年约1.5亿只。

sentences.en: Meta owns about half the smart glasses business, but it hasn't provided them a platform they can control that has enough penetration to make a difference.But wait it gets worse: since Meta hasn't got a platform comparable to a mainstream PC or mobile device OS, the Meta glasses business is vulnerable and subscale, as impressive as owning half the TAM is.
sentences.zh: Meta 拥有大约一半的智能眼镜业务，但它并没有为他们提供一个可以控制的平台，该平台具有足够的渗透力以产生影响。但等一下，情况会变得更糟：由于 Meta 没有一个与主流 PC 或移动设备操作系统相媲美的平台，Meta 眼镜业务很脆弱且规模较小，就像拥有一半的 TAM 一样令人印象深刻。

sentences.en: Since Meta glasses aren't a companion to an existing platform (not even AndroidAR, even though they run Android) they will become a second tier choice as soon as Apple or Samsung ship a smart glasses product.
sentences.zh: 由于 Meta 眼镜不是现有平台的伴侣（甚至不是 AndroidAR，尽管它们运行 Android），一旦苹果或三星推出智能眼镜产品，它们就会成为第二梯队的选择。

blocks.html_en:
I'm thinking Apple has been really smart in their AI strategy here.

It seems a mistake to make unprecedentedly large capital expenditures, in a very very crowded space, without much evidence of a moat. Presumably people thought the moat would be singularity-like self-improvement of AI, but the singularity is merely a religious concept, and nobody should take religious myth as fact, it's merely narrative for orientation and inspiration.

sentences.en: I'm thinking Apple has been really smart in their AI strategy here.It seems a mistake to make unprecedentedly large capital expenditures, in a very very crowded space, without much evidence of a moat.
sentences.zh: 我认为苹果公司的人工智能战略非常聪明。在一个非常非常拥挤的空间里，在没有太多护城河证据的情况下，进行前所未有的巨额资本支出，这似乎是一个错误。

blocks.html_en:
Their strategy to let Siri stagnate for 15 years and let everyone else take that market? Their strategy to put a bunch of not ready for consumer use AI features on their devices and then roll them back?

They just have such a strong hardware + os ecosystem that they can sit on the sidelines. They'll be able to negotiate with some LLM provider at a good discount when the time is right and put harnesses around it for actual useful features.

sentences.en: Their strategy to let Siri stagnate for 15 years and let everyone else take that market?
sentences.zh: 他们的策略是让 Siri 停滞 15 年，让其他人占领这个市场？

sentences.en: Their strategy to put a bunch of not ready for consumer use AI features on their devices and then roll them back?They just have such a strong hardware + os ecosystem that they can sit on the sidelines.
sentences.zh: 他们的策略是在他们的设备上放置一堆尚未准备好供消费者使用的人工智能功能，然后将其回滚？他们只是拥有如此强大的硬件+操作系统生态系统，以至于他们可以袖手旁观。

sentences.en: They'll be able to negotiate with some LLM provider at a good discount when the time is right and put harnesses around it for actual useful features.
sentences.zh: 当时机成熟时，他们将能够以优惠的价格与一些法学硕士提供商进行谈判，并为其提供实际有用的功能。

> Their strategy to let Siri stagnate for 15 years and let everyone else take that market?

What have they lost by doing this? Did anyone switch from iPhones to something else?

Google's Assistant was and is better than Siri's. How many switched to Android because of it?

sentences.en: How many switched to Android because of it?
sentences.zh: 有多少人因此转而使用 Android？

blocks.html_en:
I was talking more about massive investments in compute and model development. Voice assistants are barely AI, but real AI might help make better ones in the future. Similarly, I think Apple's failed attempts to add AI to macOS and iOS are a huge justification of avoiding capital investments in compute and AI frontier models.

There's two separate things here: 1) the underlying models, and 2) how they are applied to real world tasks. Both are changing at tremendous speed. The underlying models, 1), can be purchased as a component, so there's little advantage to vertical integration, as long as there's a robust competitive market, and boy is there ever. And Apple was never going to be the big B2B provider for LLM compute and models, such sales are just not in their corporate DNA.

For 2), application of AI in Apple's products, nobody knows how to do that correctly yet, and it's going to a wild few years of people trying things and failing before something comes to pass. Waiting to see where to actually find a competitive edge totally makes sense. Microsoft's Copilot bungling was far worse than Apple AI's bungling, for example. I can't even get access to Microsoft Office anymore, it seems, I'm instead confronted with a dumb chat interface that doesn't even know how to launch a new document or open existing ones. At least Apple never destroyed their brand like that...

sentences.en: I was talking more about massive investments in compute and model development.
sentences.zh: 我更多地谈论的是在计算和模型开发方面的大规模投资。

sentences.en: Voice assistants are barely AI, but real AI might help make better ones in the future.
sentences.zh: 语音助手几乎算不上人工智能，但真正的人工智能可能有助于在未来创造出更好的语音助手。

sentences.en: Similarly, I think Apple's failed attempts to add AI to macOS and iOS are a huge justification of avoiding capital investments in compute and AI frontier models.There's two separate things here: 1) the underlying models, and 2) how they are applied to real world tasks.
sentences.zh: 同样，我认为苹果在 macOS 和 iOS 中添加人工智能的失败尝试是避免在计算和人工智能前沿模型上进行资本投资的一个巨大理由。这里有两个独立的东西：1）底层模型，2）它们如何应用于现实世界的任务。

sentences.en: Both are changing at tremendous speed.
sentences.zh: 两者都在以惊人的速度发生变化。

sentences.en: The underlying models, 1), can be purchased as a component, so there's little advantage to vertical integration, as long as there's a robust competitive market, and boy is there ever.
sentences.zh: 底层模型 1) 可以作为组件购买，因此只要存在强大的竞争市场，垂直整合就没有什么优势，而且男孩永远都在那里。

sentences.en: And Apple was never going to be the big B2B provider for LLM compute and models, such sales are just not in their corporate DNA.For 2), application of AI in Apple's products, nobody knows how to do that correctly yet, and it's going to a wild few years of people trying things and failing before something comes to pass.
sentences.zh: 苹果永远不会成为 LLM 计算和模型的大型 B2B 提供商，这样的销售根本不存在于他们的企业 DNA 中。对于 2），人工智能在苹果产品中的应用，还没有人知道如何正确地做到这一点，而且在事情发生之前，人们会经历几年的疯狂尝试和失败。

sentences.en: Waiting to see where to actually find a competitive edge totally makes sense.
sentences.zh: 等待看看在哪里真正找到竞争优势是完全有道理的。

sentences.en: Microsoft's Copilot bungling was far worse than Apple AI's bungling, for example.
sentences.zh: 例如，微软 Copilot 的失误比苹果 AI 的失误要严重得多。

sentences.en: I can't even get access to Microsoft Office anymore, it seems, I'm instead confronted with a dumb chat interface that doesn't even know how to launch a new document or open existing ones.
sentences.zh: 我什至无法再访问 Microsoft Office，似乎我面临的是一个愚蠢的聊天界面，它甚至不知道如何启动新文档或打开现有文档。

sentences.en: At least Apple never destroyed their brand like that...
sentences.zh: 至少苹果从来没有像那样毁掉他们的品牌......

blocks.html_en:
I was talking more about massive investments in compute and model development. Voice assistants are barely AI, but real AI might help make better ones in the future. Similarly, I think Apple's failed attempts to add AI to macOS and iOS are a huge justification of avoiding capital investments in compute and AI frontier models.

There's two separate things here: 1) the underlying models, and 2) how they are applied to real world tasks. Both are changing at tremendous speed. The underlying models, 1), can be purchased as a component, so there's little advantage to vertical integration, as long as there's a robust competitive market, and boy is there ever. And Apple was never going to be the big B2B provider for LLM compute and models, such sales are just not in their corporate DNA.

For 2), application of AI in Apple's products, nobody knows how to do that correctly yet, and it's going to a wild few years of people trying things and failing before something comes to pass. Waiting to see where to actually find a competitive edge totally makes sense. Microsoft's Copilot bungling was far worse than Apple AI's bungling, for example. I can't even get access to Microsoft Office anymore, it seems, I'm instead confronted with a dumb chat interface that doesn't even know how to launch a new document or open existing ones. At least Apple never destroyed their brand like that...

sentences.zh: 语音助手几乎算不上人工智能，但真正的人工智能可能有助于在未来创造出更好的语音助手。

blocks.html_en:
I think that's precisely what the previous commenter is saying. Sit on the side lines, and let other people bloody themselves up.

Similar in a way to dot com. It's not to say ML won't have practical application in the future, but the likelihood that it will have specifically this form is low and worth waiting until the dust settles and a more commonly accepted utility presents itself.

If AI/ML were monstrously useful in its current form the companies pushing it would not need to be hawking products; people would be bashing their doors down. I think that's why in areas where it's more directly applied to a known problem set (like Pharma research, and I'm hoping someone with Pharma expertise can pipe up here) there has been more natural pickup.

Coming from trading and markets, ML has been a part of the mix in quantitative strategies for...well, nearly 20 years (by definition I suppose). Spaces with obvious utility will see rapid adoption. Worth waiting that out, honestly.

sentences.en: I think that's precisely what the previous commenter is saying.
sentences.zh: 我认为这正是之前评论者所说的。

sentences.en: Sit on the side lines, and let other people bloody themselves up.Similar in a way to dot com.
sentences.zh: 坐在一边，让其他人血淋淋的。在某种程度上类似于互联网。

sentences.en: It's not to say ML won't have practical application in the future, but the likelihood that it will have specifically this form is low and worth waiting until the dust settles and a more commonly accepted utility presents itself.If AI/ML were monstrously useful in its current form the companies pushing it would not need to be hawking products; people would be bashing their doors down.
sentences.zh: 这并不是说机器学习在未来不会有实际应用，而是说它以这种形式出现的可能性很低，值得等待，直到尘埃落定，更普遍接受的实用程序出现。如果人工智能/机器学习以其当前的形式非常有用，那么推动它的公司就不需要兜售产品；而是需要等待。人们会破门而入。

sentences.en: I think that's why in areas where it's more directly applied to a known problem set (like Pharma research, and I'm hoping someone with Pharma expertise can pipe up here) there has been more natural pickup.Coming from trading and markets, ML has been a part of the mix in quantitative strategies for...well, nearly 20 years (by definition I suppose).
sentences.zh: 我认为这就是为什么在它更直接应用于已知问题集的领域（例如制药研究，我希望具有制药专业知识的人可以在这里发言）出现了更自然的增长。来自交易和市场，机器学习已经成为定量策略组合的一部分……嗯，近 20 年了（根据我的定义）。

sentences.en: Spaces with obvious utility will see rapid adoption.
sentences.zh: 具有明显实用性的空间将得到快速采用。

sentences.en: Worth waiting that out, honestly.
sentences.zh: 老实说，值得等待。

blocks.html_en: The point was that one rational approach is that it's OK to not be top ranked in a market that has significant medium-term profitability issues. Apple has no problem creating new markets, but not if being top ranked requires years and years and years without return on investment after launch, and large subsidies. Not to say Apple hasn't failed at creating new markets either, or anything like that.

sentences.en: The point was that one rational approach is that it's OK to not be top ranked in a market that has significant medium-term profitability issues.
sentences.zh: 关键在于，一种理性的做法是，在存在重大中期盈利问题的市场中，不排名第一也没关系。

sentences.en: Apple has no problem creating new markets, but not if being top ranked requires years and years and years without return on investment after launch, and large subsidies.
sentences.zh: 苹果公司在开拓新市场方面没有任何问题，但如果要成为第一名需要年复一年地推出产品后没有投资回报，而且还需要大量补贴，那么苹果公司就没有问题了。

sentences.en: Not to say Apple hasn't failed at creating new markets either, or anything like that.
sentences.zh: 并不是说苹果在创造新市场或类似的事情上没有失败。

blocks.html_en: Claude is a far superior _model_ to whatever Siri uses, but in terms of the Claude app correctly transcribing speech, in my experience it's as bad or worse than Siri (which is already bad). Doesn't matter how good the model is if it misinterprets what you say.

sentences.en: Claude is a far superior _model_ to whatever Siri uses, but in terms of the Claude app correctly transcribing speech, in my experience it's as bad or worse than Siri (which is already bad).
sentences.zh: Claude 是一个远远优于 Siri 使用的模型，但就 Claude 应用程序正确转录语音而言，根据我的经验，它与 Siri 一样糟糕甚至更糟糕（这已经很糟糕了）。

sentences.en: Doesn't matter how good the model is if it misinterprets what you say.
sentences.zh: 如果模型误解了你所说的话，那么无论模型有多好。

blocks.html_en: Google and Meta's moat will be their ability to set untold billions on fire. OpenAI and Anthropic can do it for now but once the bubble pops they'll be fucked.

sentences.zh: 谷歌和 Meta 的护城河将是他们点燃无数数十亿美元的能力。

blocks.html_en:
i dont understand the concern. they are putting up great financials. you have to invest ahead of the outcome. this is just classic quarterly public company earnings BS, where public markets dont reward innovation investment. they just want crank the handle financials.

The bigger issue is on the model front, can Google compete; Gemini doesnt seem to be able to compete on the heavy expert end; they are doing well on lighter faster models.

blocks.html_en: People bought Google for the torrential free cashflow, that looks like its gone forever with this new capital intensive model. If that the case then it needs to be valued like a heavy industrial rather than a capital light tech company.

sentences.zh: 人们购买谷歌是为了获得大量的自由现金流，但随着这种新的资本密集型模式，这种情况似乎永远消失了。

sentences.zh: 如果是这样的话，那么它需要像重工业公司而不是轻资本科技公司一样被估值。

blocks.html_en: I agree Gemini's value is not at the frontier, but they are making very useful smaller models. 3.5 flash lite is super fast, cheap and token efficient and accepts any kind of input. For large, price-sensitive processing, in consolidated workflows that don't need the latest improvement, it is the best. Perhaps they should focus on improving their dominance on this business, I don't see gemini reaching claude/gpt in coding. But they could be the ones that make it possible, and economically viable, to roll out llms in large scale automation.

sentences.en: I agree Gemini's value is not at the frontier, but they are making very useful smaller models.
sentences.zh: 我同意 Gemini 的价值并不处于前沿，但他们正在制造非常有用的小型模型。

sentences.en: 3.5 flash lite is super fast, cheap and token efficient and accepts any kind of input.
sentences.zh: 3.5 flash lite 速度超快、价格便宜、令牌效率高，并且接受任何类型的输入。

sentences.en: For large, price-sensitive processing, in consolidated workflows that don't need the latest improvement, it is the best.
sentences.zh: 对于大型、价格敏感的处理，在不需要最新改进的整合工作流程中，它是最好的。

sentences.en: Perhaps they should focus on improving their dominance on this business, I don't see gemini reaching claude/gpt in coding.
sentences.zh: 也许他们应该专注于提高他们在这个行业的主导地位，我不认为双子座在编码方面达到了claude/gpt。

sentences.en: But they could be the ones that make it possible, and economically viable, to roll out llms in large scale automation.
sentences.zh: 但它们可能使在大规模自动化中推广 LLM 成为可能，并且在经济上可行。

blocks.html_en:
I've given up on Gemini. It sounds smart but most of what it tells me ends up being wrong or misleading. I might actually hand $20/mo to OpenAI. It's been far more helpful with the random collection of legal and health problems I've thrown at it. My recent comment history is going to make me come across like a shill for them but, holy crap, GPT has been doing amazing things for me at work as well.

I don't get it either... Google has so much talent yet they just can't seem to get it right.

blocks.html_en:
> you have to invest ahead of the outcome

> this is just classic quarterly public company earnings BS, where public markets dont reward innovation investment

Genuine question; but aren't these treating stocks as speculative and on vibes? One can say that these comments could be true for the first signs of cracking of dot com bubble. Sure, Web eventually succeeded but many tech giants from dot com era (AOL/Yahoo and so many more) eventually went to dust for spending too much time on the innovative bandwagon.

During the Dot-com bubble really tried to give this example but IIRC there were companies like pets.com who lost 2$ for every 1$ of sale so how a company treats its financials do matter a lot.

The market doesn't seem to reward innovation sometimes because there have been times the first persons to innovative have actually really failed to capitalize on that innovation and many extremely innovative businesses like Airlines (We can literally fly speak of innovation!) have been terrible businesses investment-wise generally speaking.

sentences.en: > you have to invest ahead of the outcome> this is just classic quarterly public company earnings BS, where public markets dont reward innovation investmentGenuine question; but aren't these treating stocks as speculative and on vibes?
sentences.zh: > 你必须在结果出现之前进行投资> 这只是典型的上市公司季度收益废话，公开市场不会奖励创新投资真正的问题；但这些难道不是把股票视为投机性的和波动性的吗？

sentences.en: One can say that these comments could be true for the first signs of cracking of dot com bubble.
sentences.zh: 可以说，这些评论对于互联网泡沫破裂的第一个迹象来说可能是正确的。

sentences.zh: 当然，网络最终取得了成功，但来自互联网时代的许多科技巨头（AOL/雅虎等）最终因在创新潮流上花费了太多时间而化为灰烬。在互联网泡沫期间，确实试图给出这个例子，但 IIRC 有像 pets.com 这样的公司，每销售 1 美元就会损失 2 美元，因此公司如何对待其财务状况确实很重要。市场有时似乎并不奖励创新，因为有时第一个创新的人实际上已经放弃了创新。确实未能利用这种创新和许多极具创新性的企业，例如航空公司（我们可以真正地谈论创新！）

sentences.en: have been terrible businesses investment-wise generally speaking.
sentences.zh: 一般来说，从投资角度来看，这些企业都是糟糕的。

blocks.html_en:
> you have to invest ahead of the outcome

> this is just classic quarterly public company earnings BS, where public markets dont reward innovation investment

Genuine question; but aren't these treating stocks as speculative and on vibes? One can say that these comments could be true for the first signs of cracking of dot com bubble. Sure, Web eventually succeeded but many tech giants from dot com era (AOL/Yahoo and so many more) eventually went to dust for spending too much time on the innovative bandwagon.

During the Dot-com bubble really tried to give this example but IIRC there were companies like pets.com who lost 2$ for every 1$ of sale so how a company treats its financials do matter a lot.

The market doesn't seem to reward innovation sometimes because there have been times the first persons to innovative have actually really failed to capitalize on that innovation and many extremely innovative businesses like Airlines (We can literally fly speak of innovation!) have been terrible businesses investment-wise generally speaking.

sentences.en: > you have to invest ahead of the outcome> this is just classic quarterly public company earnings BS, where public markets dont reward innovation investmentGenuine question; but aren't these treating stocks as speculative and on vibes?
sentences.zh: > 你必须在结果出现之前进行投资> 这只是典型的上市公司季度收益废话，公开市场不会奖励创新投资真正的问题；但这些难道不是把股票视为投机性的和波动性的吗？

blocks.html_en: They just raised $85 billion and they're sitting on a mountain of cash - if their spending didn't increase in this context, it'd be bad management. The real story here is that they have decided to spend that mountain of cash on AI CapEx.

sentences.en: The real story here is that they have decided to spend that mountain of cash on AI CapEx.
sentences.zh: 真实的情况是，他们决定将大量现金花在人工智能资本支出上。

And then the $85bn to be repaid too.

blocks.html_en:
I'm referring to the equity offering that started in June and has a second component that starts in 2026Q3. Most of this raise came from the sale of Class A and Class C stock. A fraction came from the sale of convertible stock. To my knowledge, none of this raise came from the sale of bonds.

Also, looks like I got it wrong and they've only raised $45B to date. The rest will come as part of the ATM offering program that begins in Q3.

more details here: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1652044/000119312526...

sentences.en: Most of this raise came from the sale of Class A and Class C stock.

sentences.zh: 据我所知，这些融资都不是来自债券销售。而且，看起来我错了，他们迄今为止只筹集了 $45B。

sentences.en: The rest will come as part of the ATM offering program that begins in Q3.more details here: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1652044/000119312526...

blocks.html_en:
All these big tech companies are fighting over the basics eventually like power and transformers and don't like to do anything dirty that would hurt their ESG score like getting into any sort of industrial business. Thus, the default is all that stuff that heavily bottlenecks American AI gets done in China.

If you listen to Tesla's recent conference call they are going to making solar panels all the way back to making the silicon ingots and totally vertically integrate. Elon lamented on a previous call that nobody wants to get involved in these primary industries and he has to do it all himself unless he puts his whole supply chain in China. For example, Tesla recently opened a state of the art lithium refinery in Texas cause nobody outside of China does that anymore. He's opening a new fab, because everyone else is too hesitant to expand to meet the capacity he needs.

sentences.en: All these big tech companies are fighting over the basics eventually like power and transformers and don't like to do anything dirty that would hurt their ESG score like getting into any sort of industrial business.
sentences.zh: 所有这些大型科技公司最终都在争夺电力和变压器等基础设施，并且不喜欢做任何会损害其 ESG 分数的肮脏事情，例如进入任何类型的工业业务。

sentences.en: Thus, the default is all that stuff that heavily bottlenecks American AI gets done in China.If you listen to Tesla's recent conference call they are going to making solar panels all the way back to making the silicon ingots and totally vertically integrate.
sentences.zh: 因此，默认情况下，所有那些严重阻碍美国人工智能在中国完成的事情。如果你听听特斯拉最近的电话会议，他们将继续制造太阳能电池板，直到制造硅锭并完全垂直整合。

sentences.en: Elon lamented on a previous call that nobody wants to get involved in these primary industries and he has to do it all himself unless he puts his whole supply chain in China.
sentences.zh: 埃隆在之前的一次电话会议上感叹，没有人愿意涉足这些初级产业，他必须自己做这一切，除非他把整个供应链都放在中国。

sentences.en: For example, Tesla recently opened a state of the art lithium refinery in Texas cause nobody outside of China does that anymore.
sentences.zh: 例如，特斯拉最近在德克萨斯州开设了一家最先进的锂精炼厂，因为中国以外的地区已经没有人这样做了。

sentences.en: He's opening a new fab, because everyone else is too hesitant to expand to meet the capacity he needs.
sentences.zh: 他正在开设一家新工厂，因为其他人都太犹豫是否要扩建以满足他所需的产能。

sentences.en: Everyone is already in too deep to admit that there’s a problem.
sentences.zh: 每个人都已经陷得太深，无法承认存在问题。

blocks.html_en:
The alarms in this case are that the profits and margins won’t be as high as we’ve come to expect from cloud companies.

Other than Oracle’s questionable spending spree, these big tech companies are still in very good financial positions. The enormous R&D and infrastructure spends are just feeling unusual to investors who got comparable with the unusually high margins and low costs for SaaS companies. Now they have to put a lot of that money back into the business like more normal companies.

sentences.en: The alarms in this case are that the profits and margins won’t be as high as we’ve come to expect from cloud companies.Other than Oracle’s questionable spending spree, these big tech companies are still in very good financial positions.
sentences.zh: 这种情况下的警报是，云公司的利润和利润率不会像我们预期的那么高。除了甲骨文令人怀疑的支出狂潮之外，这些大型科技公司的财务状况仍然非常良好。

sentences.en: The enormous R&D and infrastructure spends are just feeling unusual to investors who got comparable with the unusually high margins and low costs for SaaS companies.
sentences.zh: 对于那些与 SaaS 公司异常高的利润率和低成本相比较的投资者来说，巨额的研发和基础设施支出感觉很不寻常。

sentences.en: Now they have to put a lot of that money back into the business like more normal companies.
sentences.zh: 现在他们必须像更正常的公司一样将大量资金重新投入到业务中。

blocks.html_en:
If the margins aren't as high then there will be a repricing for all the massive cloud companies, which means several trillions worth of valuations to be cut from the companies.

AWS/Azure/GCP/Oracle/SpaceX/etc neoclouds... are worth a combined 10+Trillion. That going down by 50-70% is going to be insane.

sentences.zh: 下降 50-70% 将是疯狂的。

blocks.html_en: Why? GPUs are replaced every 3 to 5 years. This is going to be an ongoing operational cost forever. It will probably increase more if larger models require bigger VRAM sizes.

sentences.en: Why?
sentences.zh: 为什么？

sentences.en: GPUs are replaced every 3 to 5 years.
sentences.zh: GPU 每 3 到 5 年更换一次。

sentences.en: This is going to be an ongoing operational cost forever.
sentences.zh: 这将永远成为持续的运营成本。

sentences.en: It will probably increase more if larger models require bigger VRAM sizes.
sentences.zh: 如果较大的型号需要更大的 VRAM 大小，它可能会增加更多。

blocks.html_en: We have probably hit a limit to scaling LLMs through raw parameter count alone, at least we're not seeing the exponential pace. I personally think we'll end up with a nice sigmoid curve plateauing in the sub 10T parameter regime. The amount of tokens processed (in inference) is increasing exponentially though (I've been following open router usage stats for years and it's always been exponential). We will of course make technological advances in hardware efficiency, and model parameter efficiency, but I think a much more plausible future is that VRAM needed for loading and serving individual models will slow down or even stop. We will need more chips, and more power, as demand continues to grow of course, but the operational lifetime of GPUs today will be a lot longer than the SoTA cards from 5 years ago.

sentences.en: We have probably hit a limit to scaling LLMs through raw parameter count alone, at least we're not seeing the exponential pace.
sentences.zh: 仅通过原始参数计数来扩展 LLM 可能已经达到了极限，至少我们没有看到指数级的增长速度。

sentences.en: I personally think we'll end up with a nice sigmoid curve plateauing in the sub 10T parameter regime.
sentences.zh: 我个人认为我们最终会在低于 10T 的参数范围内得到一个很好的 sigmoid 曲线稳定状态。

sentences.en: The amount of tokens processed (in inference) is increasing exponentially though (I've been following open router usage stats for years and it's always been exponential).
sentences.zh: 不过，处理的令牌数量（推断）呈指数级增长（多年来我一直在跟踪开放路由器的使用统计数据，并且它始终呈指数级增长）。

sentences.en: We will of course make technological advances in hardware efficiency, and model parameter efficiency, but I think a much more plausible future is that VRAM needed for loading and serving individual models will slow down or even stop.
sentences.zh: 我们当然会在硬件效率和模型参数效率方面取得技术进步，但我认为更合理的未来是加载和服务单个模型所需的 VRAM 将减慢甚至停止。

sentences.en: We will need more chips, and more power, as demand continues to grow of course, but the operational lifetime of GPUs today will be a lot longer than the SoTA cards from 5 years ago.
sentences.zh: 当然，随着需求的不断增长，我们将需要更多的芯片和更多的功率，但今天 GPU 的运行寿命将比 5 年前的 SoTA 卡长得多。

blocks.html_en:
They are building new datacenters for the AI demand, so around half of this CAPEX is not for the GPU-s, and those will not be replaced every 3-5 years.

Also, TPUv2 was introduced in 2018, and still not completely retired in all regions, from accounting pov, it has been written down to 0, but they are still working.

sentences.en: They are building new datacenters for the AI demand, so around half of this CAPEX is not for the GPU-s, and those will not be replaced every 3-5 years.Also, TPUv2 was introduced in 2018, and still not completely retired in all regions, from accounting pov, it has been written down to 0, but they are still working.
sentences.zh: 他们正在为 AI 需求建设新的数据中心，因此大约一半的 CAPEX 不是用于 GPU，而且 GPU 不会每 3-5 年更换一次。此外，TPUv2 于 2018 年推出，在所有地区仍未完全退役，从会计角度看，已减记为 0，但它们仍在工作。

sentences.zh: 构建/填充数据中心的前期成本（比以前高得多）正在蚕食这些利润。

blocks.html_en:
I mean the issue is scaling, the worlds for cloud never kept getting bigger and bigger and compute scaling had stopped a while ago in the CPU space.

With AI every new generation with both massive hardware and software stack changes from Nvidia makes prior chips extremely inefficient to run, basically we are comparing an ASIC industry to a general purpose compute industry where all work loads are the same shape and size and so on.

Margins for ASIC based mining companies or ASIC solutions providers were never high, Optane and other weird solutions are niche and great for a specific category or moment in time, but they become obsolete pretty quickly.

The fear is we don't know if this Capex can stop. The worst type of fear is if this Capex will stop then what? Someone is very overpriced in this market, the cloud companies, the hardware providers or both.

I don't see how we reconcile this without a massive wave of repricing, ofc markets can stay irrational and we don't see the actual books but AI doesn't have so much revenue. Suddenly the AI token/cloud revenue won't 100x in a year or two...

Especially when intelligence will continue to get cheaper, the margin compression is a massive risk.

All the data centers for hyper scalers were a miniscule part of their story the real moat was the software layer on top otherwise Hetzner would be priced like an Amazon as well.

Something is shaky with this market I don't know what it's very opaque even as an insider working on for big tech and startups. I have no clue who falls first and which bottleneck cracks but there is not enough revenue for tokens, we will see a strong 2-3x growth in the next few years, from here which is absurd, but it's not enough, not nearly enough. If the capex keeps high and increasing.

Ofc they can stop the capex and the otherside gets repriced it's not like nvidia, micron and co aren't worth trillions.

sentences.en: I mean the issue is scaling, the worlds for cloud never kept getting bigger and bigger and compute scaling had stopped a while ago in the CPU space.With AI every new generation with both massive hardware and software stack changes from Nvidia makes prior chips extremely inefficient to run, basically we are comparing an ASIC industry to a general purpose compute industry where all work loads are the same shape and size and so on.Margins for ASIC based mining companies or ASIC solutions providers were never high, Optane and other weird solutions are niche and great for a specific category or moment in time, but they become obsolete pretty quickly.The fear is we don't know if this Capex can stop.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是问题在于扩展，云的世界从来没有变得越来越大，而计算扩展在 CPU 领域不久前就停止了。随着人工智能的出现，Nvidia 的每一代新一代硬件和软件堆栈都发生了巨大的变化，这使得之前的芯片运行效率极低，基本上我们将 ASIC 行业与通用计算行业进行比较，其中所有工作负载的形状和大小都相同等等。基于 ASIC 的采矿公司或 ASIC 解决方案提供商的利润从来都不是很高，Optane 和其他奇怪的解决方案对于特定类别来说是利基和伟大的或某个时刻，但它们很快就会过时。令人担心的是我们不知道这种资本支出是否可以停止。

sentences.en: The worst type of fear is if this Capex will stop then what?
sentences.zh: 最糟糕的恐惧是如果资本支出停止了怎么办？

sentences.en: Someone is very overpriced in this market, the cloud companies, the hardware providers or both.I don't see how we reconcile this without a massive wave of repricing, ofc markets can stay irrational and we don't see the actual books but AI doesn't have so much revenue.
sentences.zh: 在这个市场上，云公司、硬件提供商或两者都定价过高。我不知道如果没有大规模的重新定价浪潮，我们如何协调这一点，ofc 市场可能会保持非理性，我们看不到实际的书籍，但人工智能没有那么多收入。

sentences.en: Suddenly the AI token/cloud revenue won't 100x in a year or two...Especially when intelligence will continue to get cheaper, the margin compression is a massive risk.All the data centers for hyper scalers were a miniscule part of their story the real moat was the software layer on top otherwise Hetzner would be priced like an Amazon as well.Something is shaky with this market I don't know what it's very opaque even as an insider working on for big tech and startups.
sentences.zh: 突然间，人工智能代币/云收入在一两年内不会增长 100 倍……特别是当智能将继续变得更便宜时，利润压缩是一个巨大的风险。超级缩放器的所有数据中心只是他们故事的一小部分，真正的护城河是顶部的软件层，否则 Hetzner 的定价也会像亚马逊一样。这个市场有些不稳定，我不知道它是非常不透明的，即使作为为大型科技和初创公司工作的内部人士。

sentences.en: I have no clue who falls first and which bottleneck cracks but there is not enough revenue for tokens, we will see a strong 2-3x growth in the next few years, from here which is absurd, but it's not enough, not nearly enough.
sentences.zh: 我不知道谁先倒下，哪个瓶颈被打破，但代币没有足够的收入，我们将在未来几年看到强劲的 2-3 倍增长，从这里开始，这是荒谬的，但这还不够，还远远不够。

sentences.en: If the capex keeps high and increasing.Ofc they can stop the capex and the otherside gets repriced it's not like nvidia, micron and co aren't worth trillions.
sentences.zh: 如果资本支出保持高位并不断增加。OFC 他们可以停止资本支出，而另一方则重新定价，这不像英伟达、美光和公司不值数万亿美元。

> Everyone is in too deep to now admit that there’s a problem

blocks.html_en:
This assumes they do not have to increase prices to be profitable, and that they will continue to have customers when customers can switch to open models at similar performance.

As an analogy, Uber could crank up rates after the VC growth play was over to stoke revenue and profits because they have a duopoly with Lyft. LLM consumers can switch to Kimi models fairly trivially today, and whatever the frontier open model landscape looks like later. Model training and development is expensive, self hosted inference on open models not so much.

https://www.wheresyoured.at/the-openai-bubble/ has the math.

(a component of my work is currently building scaffolding so our organization can swap out commercial inference providers for on prem inference infra to derisk against the eventual rug pull when the math gets icky for LLM providers, while consuming as much subsidized tokens as we can until then, when it makes sense to use tokens for work)

sentences.en: This assumes they do not have to increase prices to be profitable, and that they will continue to have customers when customers can switch to open models at similar performance.As an analogy, Uber could crank up rates after the VC growth play was over to stoke revenue and profits because they have a duopoly with Lyft.
sentences.zh: 这是假设他们不必提高价格来盈利，并且当客户可以转向性能相似的开放模式时，他们将继续拥有客户。打个比方，Uber 可以在风投增长游戏结束后提高费率，以刺激收入和利润，因为他们与 Lyft 形成双寡头垄断。

sentences.zh: 如今，LLM 消费者可以相当简单地转向 Kimi 模型，无论以后的前沿开放模型前景如何。

sentences.en: Model training and development is expensive, self hosted inference on open models not so much.https://www.wheresyoured.at/the-openai-bubble/ has the math.(a component of my work is currently building scaffolding so our organization can swap out commercial inference providers for on prem inference infra to derisk against the eventual rug pull when the math gets icky for LLM providers, while consuming as much subsidized tokens as we can until then, when it makes sense to use tokens for work)
sentences.zh: 模型训练和开发成本高昂，开放模型上的自托管推理则没有那么多。https://www.wheresyoured.at/the-openai-bubble/ 有数学。（我工作的一个组成部分目前正在构建脚手架，以便我们的组织可以将商业推理提供商替换为本地推理基础设施，以在 LLM 提供商的数学变得棘手时避免最终的风险，同时在有意义的时候消耗尽可能多的补贴代币使用代币进行工作）

blocks.html_en:
The question will be whether customers can switch.

Can you install a near-SOTA model on a cluster in a data center? Of course. Compliance and operations are the sticking points. I work in healthcare IT, and it's amazing how tight the data compliance requirements are. I can't have someone in Canada look at prod data. If we told hospitals that we were handing off PHI/PII to Chinese models, they'd end our relationship due to the long history China has of hacking Western networks and computers. They don't care how open and cheap things are.

Then, you have to keep up-to-date on the latest technology and right-size things in a very fluid market. If you sign a contract for hosting the model on a data center that's running what the SOTA is now in hardware, and someone comes through with a data center hardware or software product that makes that data center contract a disadvantage (maybe it's too expensive and the other party won't budge on the price), you might have to factor that into your offering's price, and that could put you at a disadvantage in your marketplace.

Google, MS, etc. all want to leverage the cloud model to make this be less of an issue for you, for a price. They have the ability to update you with the SOTA stuff in the data centers, because they're the ones driving that SOTA. They can say they host in the US and develop most of their stuff in the US.

Will that be enough of a moat?

Probably not for the levels of spending that are happening now, but over the long term, probably.

sentences.en: The question will be whether customers can switch.Can you install a near-SOTA model on a cluster in a data center?
sentences.zh: 问题是客户是否可以切换。您可以在数据中心的集群上安装接近 SOTA 的模型吗？

sentences.en: Of course.
sentences.zh: 当然。

sentences.en: Compliance and operations are the sticking points.
sentences.zh: 合规和运营是症结所在。

sentences.en: I work in healthcare IT, and it's amazing how tight the data compliance requirements are.
sentences.zh: 我从事医疗保健 IT 工作，令人惊讶的是数据合规性要求如此严格。

sentences.en: I can't have someone in Canada look at prod data.
sentences.zh: 我无法让加拿大的人查看产品数据。

sentences.en: If we told hospitals that we were handing off PHI/PII to Chinese models, they'd end our relationship due to the long history China has of hacking Western networks and computers.
sentences.zh: 如果我们告诉医院我们正在将 PHI/PII 交给中国模型，他们就会结束我们的关系，因为中国长期以来一直在攻击西方网络和计算机。

sentences.en: They don't care how open and cheap things are.Then, you have to keep up-to-date on the latest technology and right-size things in a very fluid market.
sentences.zh: 他们不在乎东西有多开放和便宜。然后，你必须在一个非常流动的市场中了解最新的技术和合适的尺寸的东西。

sentences.en: If you sign a contract for hosting the model on a data center that's running what the SOTA is now in hardware, and someone comes through with a data center hardware or software product that makes that data center contract a disadvantage (maybe it's too expensive and the other party won't budge on the price), you might have to factor that into your offering's price, and that could put you at a disadvantage in your marketplace.Google, MS, etc. all want to leverage the cloud model to make this be less of an issue for you, for a price.
sentences.zh: 如果您签署了一份在运行 SOTA 现在硬件的数据中心上托管模型的合同，并且有人提供了使该数据中心合同处于劣势的数据中心硬件或软件产品（可能太贵并且另一方不会在价格上让步），您可能必须将其纳入您的产品价格中，这可能会使您在市场中处于劣势。Google、MS 等都希望利用云模型来减少这对您来说是一个问题，价格。

sentences.en: They have the ability to update you with the SOTA stuff in the data centers, because they're the ones driving that SOTA.
sentences.zh: 他们有能力向您更新数据中心的 SOTA 内容，因为他们是推动 SOTA 的人。

sentences.en: They can say they host in the US and develop most of their stuff in the US.Will that be enough of a moat?Probably not for the levels of spending that are happening now, but over the long term, probably.
sentences.zh: 他们可以说他们在美国托管并在美国开发大部分产品。这足够成为护城河吗？可能不是针对现在的支出水平，但从长远来看，可能是这样。

blocks.html_en:
The question will be whether customers can switch.

Can you install a near-SOTA model on a cluster in a data center? Of course. Compliance and operations are the sticking points. I work in healthcare IT, and it's amazing how tight the data compliance requirements are. I can't have someone in Canada look at prod data. If we told hospitals that we were handing off PHI/PII to Chinese models, they'd end our relationship due to the long history China has of hacking Western networks and computers. They don't care how open and cheap things are.

Then, you have to keep up-to-date on the latest technology and right-size things in a very fluid market. If you sign a contract for hosting the model on a data center that's running what the SOTA is now in hardware, and someone comes through with a data center hardware or software product that makes that data center contract a disadvantage (maybe it's too expensive and the other party won't budge on the price), you might have to factor that into your offering's price, and that could put you at a disadvantage in your marketplace.

Google, MS, etc. all want to leverage the cloud model to make this be less of an issue for you, for a price. They have the ability to update you with the SOTA stuff in the data centers, because they're the ones driving that SOTA. They can say they host in the US and develop most of their stuff in the US.

Will that be enough of a moat?

Probably not for the levels of spending that are happening now, but over the long term, probably.

blocks.html_en:
How long will a SOTA model be necessary? If day to day work can be achieved on an open weight model, the most evaporates overnight.

Look at any computer in a big company. It isn't the fastest on the market, nor will it have the most RAM or largest monitor or fanciest keyboard. It is good enough at a good enough price point. Once it becomes possible and cheaper to host your own good enough open weight models, with all the benefits of keeping data internal to the company, then the big providers are cooked, so to speak.

sentences.zh: 它以足够好的价格点已经足够好了。

blocks.html_en:
> How long will a SOTA model be necessary? If day to day work can be achieved on an open weight model, the most evaporates overnight.

Depends on the advantage it gives people and marketing of that advantage. You'd be surprised at how overpowered the average workplace laptop is. Each company I've been at has had at least some people who do non-technical roles using high-end hardware. Why? Because the account executive wants the fast machine and they get what they want.

You can apply the same to GenAI. Humans are notoriously bad at estimating actual needs when it comes to resource consumption. Best to have it and not need it than need it and not have it, especially if your competition just shelled out for SOTA.

And that's not even taking into consideration regulatory and customer concerns about where the AI you're serving requests with came from.

sentences.en: > How long will a SOTA model be necessary?
sentences.zh: > SOTA 模型需要多长时间？

sentences.en: If day to day work can be achieved on an open weight model, the most evaporates overnight.Depends on the advantage it gives people and marketing of that advantage.
sentences.zh: 如果日常工作可以在开放重量模型上完成，那么大部分工作都会在一夜之间蒸发。取决于它为人们带来的优势以及该优势的营销。

sentences.en: You'd be surprised at how overpowered the average workplace laptop is.
sentences.zh: 您会对普通工作场所笔记本电脑的强大性能感到惊讶。

sentences.en: Each company I've been at has had at least some people who do non-technical roles using high-end hardware.
sentences.zh: 我工作过的每家公司都至少有一些人使用高端硬件担任非技术角色。

sentences.en: Why?
sentences.zh: 为什么？

sentences.en: Because the account executive wants the fast machine and they get what they want.You can apply the same to GenAI.
sentences.zh: 因为客户主管想要快速的机器，并且他们得到了他们想要的。你可以将同样的方法应用于 GenAI。

sentences.en: Humans are notoriously bad at estimating actual needs when it comes to resource consumption.
sentences.zh: 众所周知，人类在资源消耗方面不善于估计实际需求。

sentences.en: Best to have it and not need it than need it and not have it, especially if your competition just shelled out for SOTA.And that's not even taking into consideration regulatory and customer concerns about where the AI you're serving requests with came from.
sentences.zh: 拥有它但不需要它比需要它但没有它更好，特别是如果你的竞争对手刚刚为 SOTA 付出了代价。这甚至没有考虑监管和客户对你所处理请求的人工智能来自何处的担忧。

blocks.html_en:
Execs getting what they want doesn't mean they let everyone have the same thing. It's far more likely that everyone else is using lesser equipment.

We have already seen tech workers at big name companies get whiplash from "leaderboards showing people using the most tokens!" as a good thing one month to being pressured to using fewer tokens a month later.

sentences.en: Execs getting what they want doesn't mean they let everyone have the same thing.
sentences.zh: 高管得到他们想要的东西并不意味着他们让每个人都拥有同样的东西。

sentences.en: It's far more likely that everyone else is using lesser equipment.We have already seen tech workers at big name companies get whiplash from "leaderboards showing people using the most tokens!"
sentences.zh: 更有可能的是，其他人都使用较少的设备。我们已经看到知名公司的技术人员因“排行榜显示人们使用最多的代币！”而受到鞭打！

sentences.en: as a good thing one month to being pressured to using fewer tokens a month later.
sentences.zh: 一个月后被迫使用更少的代币是一件好事。
