> Execs getting what they want doesn't mean they let everyone have the same thing. It's far more likely that everyone else is using lesser equipment.

You'd be surprised. I've seen people request upgrades and get them. Not even execs, just employees. Even the average devices are probably overpowered these days. Chromebooks could do the vast majority of work in a corporate environment. Try getting workers to accept them, though.

Regardless, there are probably enough reasons to use proprietary Western AI for the time being, particularly in regulated industries, that poop won't completely hit the fan. Particularly if the CTOs start searching the phrase "Operation Aurora".

> We have already seen tech workers at big name companies get whiplash from "leaderboards showing people using the most tokens!" as a good thing one month to being pressured to using fewer tokens a month later.

Indeed. What do you do when you want a group of people to do something that they'd be otherwise adverse to doing at all? You rank them on it and let them get in a competition over who can do it the most. That's what tokenmaxxing was about: getting people to use the AI at all. Now that the people are using AI, we move onto another objective, which is getting them to use the tokens efficiently. The trick is measuring that efficiency.

sentences.en: > Execs getting what they want doesn't mean they let everyone have the same thing.
sentences.zh: > 高管得到他们想要的东西并不意味着他们让每个人都拥有同样的东西。

sentences.en: It's far more likely that everyone else is using lesser equipment.You'd be surprised.
sentences.zh: 更有可能的是，其他人都使用较少的设备。您会感到惊讶。

sentences.en: I've seen people request upgrades and get them.
sentences.zh: 我见过有人要求升级并得到升级。

sentences.en: Not even execs, just employees.
sentences.zh: 甚至不是高管，只是员工。

sentences.en: Even the average devices are probably overpowered these days.
sentences.zh: 如今，即使是普通设备也可能不堪重负。

sentences.en: Chromebooks could do the vast majority of work in a corporate environment.
sentences.zh: Chromebook 可以在企业环境中完成绝大多数工作。

sentences.en: Try getting workers to accept them, though.Regardless, there are probably enough reasons to use proprietary Western AI for the time being, particularly in regulated industries, that poop won't completely hit the fan.
sentences.zh: 不过，请尝试让工人接受它们。无论如何，目前可能有足够的理由使用专有的西方人工智能，特别是在受监管的行业，粪便不会完全受到关注。

sentences.en: Particularly if the CTOs start searching the phrase "Operation Aurora".> We have already seen tech workers at big name companies get whiplash from "leaderboards showing people using the most tokens!"
sentences.zh: 特别是如果 CTO 开始搜索“Operation Aurora”一词。> 我们已经看到知名公司的技术人员因“排行榜显示人们使用最多的代币！”而受到鞭打！

sentences.en: as a good thing one month to being pressured to using fewer tokens a month later.Indeed.
sentences.zh: 一个月后被迫使用更少的代币是一件好事。确实如此。

sentences.en: What do you do when you want a group of people to do something that they'd be otherwise adverse to doing at all?
sentences.zh: 当你想让一群人做一些他们根本不愿意做的事情时，你会怎么做？

sentences.en: You rank them on it and let them get in a competition over who can do it the most.
sentences.zh: 你可以对他们进行排名，然后让他们参加比赛，看看谁能做得最多。

sentences.en: That's what tokenmaxxing was about: getting people to use the AI at all.
sentences.zh: 这就是 tokenmaxxing 的目的：让人们真正使用人工智能。

sentences.en: Now that the people are using AI, we move onto another objective, which is getting them to use the tokens efficiently.
sentences.zh: 既然人们正在使用人工智能，我们就转向另一个目标，即让他们有效地使用代币。

sentences.en: The trick is measuring that efficiency.
sentences.zh: 诀窍在于衡量效率。

blocks.html_en:
My primary role is cybersecurity in a regulated entity in a regulated industry, I am highly confident it is straightforward to do so based on work accomplished in only a couple of weeks. Stand up a router, stand up a Kubernetes cluster if you don't have one, stand up the necessary VMs and compute for serving inference. Two pizza team, in my experience.

Customers can switch (although we can argue the speed and pain of doing so), and the speed at which they do will be a function of cost efficiency and demonstrable value (imho). A recent example of this is Broadcom and VMware [1], for example. When motivated, it can be done. If there is no objective, measured value being delivered, the spend will be cut. If the value delivered is measured, it will be enabled at a lower cost through cost optimization measures (ie self hosting) [2].

This is all to say: there is no moat, the revenue of inference providers is volatile and not assured in any measure. Caveat emptor.

[1] https://hn.algolia.com/?dateRange=all&page=0&prefix=false&qu...

[2] Microsoft considers replacing ChatGPT and Claude with Kimi K3 to save $600M - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49022984 - July 2026

sentences.en: My primary role is cybersecurity in a regulated entity in a regulated industry, I am highly confident it is straightforward to do so based on work accomplished in only a couple of weeks.
sentences.zh: 我的主要职责是受监管行业中受监管实体的网络安全，我非常有信心根据在短短几周内完成的工作来做到这一点是很简单的。

sentences.en: Stand up a router, stand up a Kubernetes cluster if you don't have one, stand up the necessary VMs and compute for serving inference.
sentences.zh: 建立一个路由器，建立一个 Kubernetes 集群（如果没有的话），建立必要的虚拟机并进行计算以服务推理。

sentences.en: Two pizza team, in my experience.Customers can switch (although we can argue the speed and pain of doing so), and the speed at which they do will be a function of cost efficiency and demonstrable value (imho).
sentences.zh: 根据我的经验，两个披萨团队。客户可以切换（尽管我们可以争论这样做的速度和痛苦），并且他们的速度将是成本效率和可证明价值的函数（恕我直言）。

sentences.en: A recent example of this is Broadcom and VMware [1], for example.
sentences.zh: 最近的一个例子是 Broadcom 和 VMware [1]。

sentences.en: When motivated, it can be done.
sentences.zh: 当有动力时，就可以做到。

sentences.en: If there is no objective, measured value being delivered, the spend will be cut.
sentences.zh: 如果没有提供客观的、可衡量的价值，支出就会被削减。

sentences.en: If the value delivered is measured, it will be enabled at a lower cost through cost optimization measures (ie self hosting) [2].This is all to say: there is no moat, the revenue of inference providers is volatile and not assured in any measure.
sentences.zh: 如果衡量所交付的价值，则可以通过成本优化措施（即自托管）以较低的成本启用它[2]。这就是说：没有护城河，推理提供商的收入不稳定并且在任何方面都没有保证。

sentences.en: Caveat emptor.[1] https://hn.algolia.com/?dateRange=all&page=0&prefix=false&qu...[2] Microsoft considers replacing ChatGPT and Claude with Kimi K3 to save $600M - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49022984 - July 2026
sentences.zh: 买者自负。[1] https://hn.algolia.com/?dateRange=all&page=0&prefix=false&qu...[2] Microsoft 考虑用 Kimi K3 取代 ChatGPT 和 Claude，以节省 6 亿美元 - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49022984 - 2026 年 7 月

blocks.html_en:
Meanwhile, in real companies, you have to wait 2 months or more to access an API endpoint in preprod.

To setup a cross business kubernetes cluster will take 2 years with unknown results.

On Cloud, in Switzerland, you need to call Microsoft when you need new resources, so much for agility and minute infrastructure provisioning, and I heard the same for AWS.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, in real companies, you have to wait 2 months or more to access an API endpoint in preprod.To setup a cross business kubernetes cluster will take 2 years with unknown results.On Cloud, in Switzerland, you need to call Microsoft when you need new resources, so much for agility and minute infrastructure provisioning, and I heard the same for AWS.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，在现实的公司中，你必须等待 2 个月或更长时间才能访问 preprod 中的 API 端点。要设置跨业务 Kubernetes 集群将需要 2 年时间，结果未知。在云上，在瑞士，当你需要新资源时，你需要致电 Microsoft，需要敏捷性和分钟基础设施配置，我听说 AWS 也是如此。

blocks.html_en:
There's a difference between "It's straightforward to do" and "I can convince a customer to sign a contract allowing us to do it."

If the second one were as easy as the first, I wouldn't have to be online at 9:00 to deploy stuff to prod tonight; the team in India would handle it. But customers write into the contracts that only US-based employees interact with prod systems. No amount of cajoling will get them to change their minds; they have data sovereignty, international telecommunications treaties, and HIPAA compliance to worry about. So I'll be pressing buttons tonight.

Could you swap out Anthropic or OpenAI or Google or whoever's models for Kimi? Yes. They're like other software these days, they're modular. What isn't modular is regulatory and geopolitical concern.

sentences.en: There's a difference between "It's straightforward to do" and "I can convince a customer to sign a contract allowing us to do it."If the second one were as easy as the first, I wouldn't have to be online at 9:00 to deploy stuff to prod tonight; the team in India would handle it.
sentences.zh: “做起来很简单”和“我可以说服客户签署一份允许我们这样做的合同”之间是有区别的。如果第二个和第一个一样简单，那么今晚我就不必在 9:00 在线将东西部署到产品中；印度的团队会处理它。

sentences.en: But customers write into the contracts that only US-based employees interact with prod systems.
sentences.zh: 但客户在合同中写道，只有美国员工才能与产品系统交互。

sentences.en: No amount of cajoling will get them to change their minds; they have data sovereignty, international telecommunications treaties, and HIPAA compliance to worry about.
sentences.zh: 再多的哄骗也无法让他们改变主意；他们需要担心数据主权、国际电信条约和 HIPAA 合规性。

sentences.en: So I'll be pressing buttons tonight.Could you swap out Anthropic or OpenAI or Google or whoever's models for Kimi?
sentences.zh: 所以今晚我会按下按钮。你能把 Anthropic 或 OpenAI 或 Google 或任何人的模型换成 Kimi 吗？

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: They're like other software these days, they're modular.
sentences.zh: 它们就像当今的其他软件一样，是模块化的。

sentences.en: What isn't modular is regulatory and geopolitical concern.
sentences.zh: 不模块化的是监管和地缘政治问题。

blocks.html_en:
Good luck explaining to an exec that the locally hosted Chinese model definitely doesn't have a backdoor or hidden trained-in intentions.

Meanwhile the cost/benefit analysis doesn't move much even if you are paying 2x for tokens, and you don't need anything on prem.

blocks.html_en: The technology is too hard to capitalize on. It’s far more democratic than, say, an iPhone, or a search engine. Anyone can download a model to their computer and start toying with it, how do you profit off of that? Even if everyone was constantly tokenmaxxing (which we cannot, since the process gets fucked up if you let it run entirely on its own), it probably still wouldn’t be marginally profitable.

sentences.en: The technology is too hard to capitalize on.
sentences.zh: 这项技术太难利用了。

sentences.en: It’s far more democratic than, say, an iPhone, or a search engine.
sentences.zh: 它比 iPhone 或搜索引擎等民主得多。

sentences.en: Anyone can download a model to their computer and start toying with it, how do you profit off of that?
sentences.zh: 任何人都可以将模型下载到他们的计算机上并开始摆弄它，您如何从中获利？

sentences.en: Even if everyone was constantly tokenmaxxing (which we cannot, since the process gets fucked up if you let it run entirely on its own), it probably still wouldn’t be marginally profitable.
sentences.zh: 即使每个人都不断地进行代币最大化（我们不能这样做，因为如果你让它完全自行运行，这个过程就会搞砸），它可能仍然不会有边际利润。

blocks.html_en:
Ans so far, the dramatic improvements have come with an increase in API costs.

Even if, hypothetically, Fable or a Fable-class model could seriously replace some headcount, it'll only gain further traction of it's actually cheaper than hiring humans. $50/MTok is expensive. Wouldn't be unreasonable to expect somewhere between ~$3k-$5k/month/developer in spend. Cheaper than a Junior in the HCoL areas (in the US), but not much cheaper in lower-to-average COL areas. Most acceleration will come from having the headcount + giving said headcount $3k-$5k/month in token budget, so now it just becomes a very expensive dev tool rather than a headcount replacement tool.

The idea that a $30k/year API bill will replace 2 $100k developers falls part outside of SFC/NYC. No CFO of a mid-market company in a LCOL area is signing off on $3k/month/dev API bills. They'll just hire juniors and cap their spend at $200/month.

sentences.en: Ans so far, the dramatic improvements have come with an increase in API costs.Even if, hypothetically, Fable or a Fable-class model could seriously replace some headcount, it'll only gain further traction of it's actually cheaper than hiring humans. $50/MTok is expensive.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，显着的改进伴随着 API 成本的增加。即使假设 Fable 或 Fable 类模型可以严重取代一些员工，它也只会获得进一步的吸引力，因为它实际上比雇用人类更便宜。 50 美元/MTok 很贵。

sentences.en: Wouldn't be unreasonable to expect somewhere between ~$3k-$5k/month/developer in spend.
sentences.zh: 预计每个开发人员每月的支出约为 3,000 美元至 5,000 美元之间，这并不是不合理的。

sentences.en: Cheaper than a Junior in the HCoL areas (in the US), but not much cheaper in lower-to-average COL areas.
sentences.zh: 在 HCoL 地区（美国）比 Junior 便宜，但在低于平均水平的 COL 地区便宜不了多少。

sentences.en: Most acceleration will come from having the headcount + giving said headcount $3k-$5k/month in token budget, so now it just becomes a very expensive dev tool rather than a headcount replacement tool.The idea that a $30k/year API bill will replace 2 $100k developers falls part outside of SFC/NYC.
sentences.zh: 大多数加速将来自于拥有员工数量+为员工提供每月 3000-5000 美元的代币预算，所以现在它只是成为一个非常昂贵的开发工具，而不是员工替代工具。每年 3 万美元的 API 账单将取代 2 个 10 万美元的开发人员的想法不属于 SFC/NYC 的范畴。

sentences.en: No CFO of a mid-market company in a LCOL area is signing off on $3k/month/dev API bills.
sentences.zh: LCOL 领域中端市场公司的首席财务官没有签署每月 3000 美元/开发 API 账单。

sentences.en: They'll just hire juniors and cap their spend at $200/month.
sentences.zh: 他们只会雇佣初级员工，并将他们的支出上限控制在每月 200 美元。

blocks.html_en:
Ans so far, the dramatic improvements have come with an increase in API costs.

Even if, hypothetically, Fable or a Fable-class model could seriously replace some headcount, it'll only gain further traction of it's actually cheaper than hiring humans. $50/MTok is expensive. Wouldn't be unreasonable to expect somewhere between ~$3k-$5k/month/developer in spend. Cheaper than a Junior in the HCoL areas (in the US), but not much cheaper in lower-to-average COL areas. Most acceleration will come from having the headcount + giving said headcount $3k-$5k/month in token budget, so now it just becomes a very expensive dev tool rather than a headcount replacement tool.

The idea that a $30k/year API bill will replace 2 $100k developers falls part outside of SFC/NYC. No CFO of a mid-market company in a LCOL area is signing off on $3k/month/dev API bills. They'll just hire juniors and cap their spend at $200/month.

sentences.en: Ans so far, the dramatic improvements have come with an increase in API costs.Even if, hypothetically, Fable or a Fable-class model could seriously replace some headcount, it'll only gain further traction of it's actually cheaper than hiring humans. $50/MTok is expensive.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，显着的改进伴随着 API 成本的增加。即使假设 Fable 或 Fable 类模型可以严重取代一些员工，它也只会获得进一步的吸引力，因为它实际上比雇用人类更便宜。 50 美元/MTok 很贵。

blocks.html_en:
IIRC, the files for Toy Story 2 were accidentally deleted during production, and the film was only saved because someone on maternity leave had a backup at home.

Turns out you can fuck up self hosting too.

blocks.html_en: Yes, Google randomly deleted UniSuper for basically the same reason they randomly ban individual customers: they don't care. Relying on them for anything is a huge mistake.

sentences.en: Relying on them for anything is a huge mistake.
sentences.zh: 任何事情都依赖他们是一个巨大的错误。

blocks.html_en:
Source? Has Anthropic's annualized revenue not quadrupled in the last 7 months? And OpenAI's annualized revenue quadrupled since January 2025? Which is only unimpressive by comparison to Anthropic's meteoric revenue growth

I'd be with you if you claimed that the revenue hasn't translated into substantial profits. Being able to spend a lot of money to get less money back is not that impressive. But revenue by itself is on a dramatic rise as capabilities improve

sentences.en: Source?
sentences.zh: 来源？

sentences.en: Has Anthropic's annualized revenue not quadrupled in the last 7 months?
sentences.zh: Anthropic 的年化收入在过去 7 个月里不是翻了两番吗？

sentences.en: And OpenAI's annualized revenue quadrupled since January 2025?
sentences.zh: 自 2025 年 1 月以来，OpenAI 的年化收入翻了两番？

sentences.en: Which is only unimpressive by comparison to Anthropic's meteoric revenue growthI'd be with you if you claimed that the revenue hasn't translated into substantial profits.
sentences.zh: 与 Anthropic 飞速的收入增长相比，这只是不起眼的事情，如果你声称收入没有转化为可观的利润，我会同意你的观点。

sentences.en: Being able to spend a lot of money to get less money back is not that impressive.
sentences.zh: 能够花很多钱换回更少的钱，这并不令人印象深刻。

sentences.en: But revenue by itself is on a dramatic rise as capabilities improve
sentences.zh: 但随着能力的提高，收入本身正在急剧增长

blocks.html_en:
I know it's easy to forget, and InsideOutSanta kind of anchored the conversation on "revenue", but profit is ultimately what matters. Back when Silicon Valley was merely insane rather than bat-guano crazy insane, it was commonly observed that it's not hard to build a business around selling a dollar for 95 cents. The point being that it doesn't necessarily mean much when you have a business doing that, because of course the demand will be insane. It doesn't mean you have a viable business. You don't know you have a viable business until you transition to selling a dollar for $1.03. Many a VC-funded business that looked successful, even wildly successful, has run aground on that transition, or at least, suddenly stopped looking so wildly successful.

If AI-related expenses are also growing exponentially, and they are growing exponentially faster, it doesn't matter that revenue is growing exponentially.

The AI funding has also now absolutely baked in exponential growth of expenses, because that's how debt works. A slow exponential, hopefully, but an exponential none-the-less.

Something Hacker News needs to be periodically reminded of is that we are the field getting the most out of AI, and it's not even close. That's great for us. But the stocks aren't priced for "a pretty nice coding tool". They're priced for every field in the world getting even more value out of this than our field is getting now. That is, frankly, not happening anywhere near fast enough for the spending and stock valuations. When you don't have all the engineering guardrails that are present in software engineering [1], suddenly the AI is, ahem, exponentially less useful.

As I say in that post, watch your AI actually doing something, even the frontier models. Watch the thinking traces. Watch how many times they bang into a guardrail of some sort; a failing test, a failing compile, a linter failure, a bash script that doesn't work, all those things. How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if the first time it banged into a guard rail it was done and you had to stop using it for that task? How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if instead it silently failed and just proceeded forward with errors that you lack the infrastructure to easily detect? In the first case, it would be fairly modest, almost certainly not worth the money, and in the second, it would be worth paying to not use.

Even in our field, while the rate of code output has increased substantially, the rate of value generation increase has been quite a bit more modest. I have observed, and heard from a number of other places, that while my own output has increased somewhat we still generally can't plan on being able to work with other teams at much faster a rate than we used to.

There's a viable business here but I can't see how all these companies expect to be returning all this revenue in any financially sensible period of time. They're all spending like if only they spend enough they can own about %900 of the market in three years. They can't all do that, even accounting for "AI makes the market bigger".

And they're wildly vulnerable to some new solution coming out that obsoletes all this spending, like an ASIC that starts running a popular model directly (especially if model capabilities plateau), meaning that all this nVidia GPU spending is so much dead silicon. Or someone comes out with a much more efficient way to train models. There has to be some insight we're missing; humans do not learn what they do by having the entire contents of the Internet poured through their head hundreds of times over. We are far more efficient with our training data. What if someone works out a solution to that and we don't need to spend billions on GPUs but only millions? The whole spending proposition could collapse overnight and the companies that suddenly have three orders of magnitude too much hardware and the debt to match would be up a creek without a paddle.

[1]: https://jerf.org/iri/post/2026/programming_is_engineering/

sentences.zh: 整个支出方案可能会在一夜之间崩溃，那些突然拥有三个数量级的硬件和与之相匹配的债务的公司将陷入无桨的小溪。[1]：https://jerf.org/iri/post/2026/programming_is_engineering/

blocks.html_en: I know it's easy to forget, and InsideOutSanta kind of anchored the conversation on "revenue", but profit is ultimately what matters. Back when Silicon Valley was merely insane rather than bat-guano crazy insane, it was commonly observed that it's not hard to build a business around selling a dollar for 95 cents. The point being that it doesn't necessarily mean much when you have a business doing that, because of course the demand will be insane. It doesn't mean you have a viable business. You don't know you have a viable business until you transition to selling a dollar for $1.03. Many a VC-funded business that looked successful, even wildly successful, has run aground on that transition, or at least, suddenly stopped looking so wildly successful.

sentences.en: I know it's easy to forget, and InsideOutSanta kind of anchored the conversation on "revenue", but profit is ultimately what matters.
sentences.zh: 我知道这很容易被忘记，《InsideOutSanta》将谈话重点放在了“收入”上，但利润最终才是最重要的。

sentences.en: Back when Silicon Valley was merely insane rather than bat-guano crazy insane, it was commonly observed that it's not hard to build a business around selling a dollar for 95 cents.
sentences.zh: 当硅谷还只是疯狂而不是蝙蝠鸟粪疯狂时，人们普遍认为，以 1 美元卖 95 美分来建立一家企业并不难。

sentences.en: The point being that it doesn't necessarily mean much when you have a business doing that, because of course the demand will be insane.
sentences.zh: 关键是，当你有一家企业这样做时，这并不一定意味着什么，因为需求当然会很疯狂。

sentences.en: It doesn't mean you have a viable business.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着您拥有可行的业务。

sentences.en: You don't know you have a viable business until you transition to selling a dollar for $1.03.
sentences.zh: 在您转变为以 1.03 美元出售一美元之前，您并不知道自己的业务可行。

sentences.en: Many a VC-funded business that looked successful, even wildly successful, has run aground on that transition, or at least, suddenly stopped looking so wildly successful.
sentences.zh: 许多风投资助的企业看起来很成功，甚至非常成功，但在转型过程中却搁浅了，或者至少突然不再看起来那么成功了。

blocks.html_en: If AI-related expenses are also growing exponentially, and they are growing exponentially faster, it doesn't matter that revenue is growing exponentially.

sentences.en: If AI-related expenses are also growing exponentially, and they are growing exponentially faster, it doesn't matter that revenue is growing exponentially.
sentences.zh: 如果与人工智能相关的支出也呈指数级增长，而且增长速度呈指数级增长，那么收入呈指数级增长就无关紧要了。

blocks.html_en: The AI funding has also now absolutely baked in exponential growth of expenses, because that's how debt works. A slow exponential, hopefully, but an exponential none-the-less.

sentences.en: The AI funding has also now absolutely baked in exponential growth of expenses, because that's how debt works.
sentences.zh: 人工智能资金现在也完全导致了支出的指数级增长，因为这就是债务的运作方式。

sentences.en: A slow exponential, hopefully, but an exponential none-the-less.
sentences.zh: 希望是一个缓慢的指数增长，但仍然是一个指数增长。

blocks.html_en: Something Hacker News needs to be periodically reminded of is that we are the field getting the most out of AI, and it's not even close. That's great for us. But the stocks aren't priced for "a pretty nice coding tool". They're priced for every field in the world getting even more value out of this than our field is getting now. That is, frankly, not happening anywhere near fast enough for the spending and stock valuations. When you don't have all the engineering guardrails that are present in software engineering [1], suddenly the AI is, ahem, exponentially less useful.

sentences.en: Something Hacker News needs to be periodically reminded of is that we are the field getting the most out of AI, and it's not even close.
sentences.zh: 《黑客新闻》需要定期提醒我们的是，我们是充分利用人工智能的领域，但距离还很遥远。

sentences.en: That's great for us.
sentences.zh: 这对我们来说很棒。

sentences.en: But the stocks aren't priced for "a pretty nice coding tool".
sentences.zh: 但这些股票的定价并不是“一个非常好的编码工具”。

sentences.en: They're priced for every field in the world getting even more value out of this than our field is getting now.
sentences.zh: 它们的定价适用于世界上的每个领域，从中获得的价值甚至比我们的领域现在获得的价值还要多。

sentences.en: That is, frankly, not happening anywhere near fast enough for the spending and stock valuations.
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，对于支出和股票估值而言，这种情况发生的速度还不够快。

sentences.en: When you don't have all the engineering guardrails that are present in software engineering [1], suddenly the AI is, ahem, exponentially less useful.
sentences.zh: 当你没有软件工程中存在的所有工程护栏时 [1]，突然间，人工智能的用处就会呈指数级下降。

blocks.html_en: As I say in that post, watch your AI actually doing something, even the frontier models. Watch the thinking traces. Watch how many times they bang into a guardrail of some sort; a failing test, a failing compile, a linter failure, a bash script that doesn't work, all those things. How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if the first time it banged into a guard rail it was done and you had to stop using it for that task? How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if instead it silently failed and just proceeded forward with errors that you lack the infrastructure to easily detect? In the first case, it would be fairly modest, almost certainly not worth the money, and in the second, it would be worth paying to not use.

sentences.en: As I say in that post, watch your AI actually doing something, even the frontier models.
sentences.zh: 正如我在那篇文章中所说，观察你的人工智能实际上在做什么，甚至是前沿模型。

sentences.en: Watch the thinking traces.
sentences.zh: 观察思维痕迹。

sentences.en: Watch how many times they bang into a guardrail of some sort; a failing test, a failing compile, a linter failure, a bash script that doesn't work, all those things.
sentences.zh: 观察他们有多少次撞到某种护栏；失败的测试、失败的编译、linter 失败、bash 脚本不起作用，所有这些事情。

sentences.en: How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if the first time it banged into a guard rail it was done and you had to stop using it for that task?
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能编码助手第一次撞到护栏时就完成了，而你不得不停止使用它来完成这项任务，那么你能从它身上获得多少价值？

sentences.en: How much value would you get out of an AI coding assistant if instead it silently failed and just proceeded forward with errors that you lack the infrastructure to easily detect?
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能编码助手默默地失败并继续执行您缺乏基础设施以轻松检测到的错误，您会从人工智能编码助手中获得多少价值？

sentences.en: In the first case, it would be fairly modest, almost certainly not worth the money, and in the second, it would be worth paying to not use.
sentences.zh: 在第一种情况下，它的价格相当适中，几乎肯定不值这个钱，而在第二种情况下，值得花钱而不使用。

blocks.html_en: Even in our field, while the rate of code output has increased substantially, the rate of value generation increase has been quite a bit more modest. I have observed, and heard from a number of other places, that while my own output has increased somewhat we still generally can't plan on being able to work with other teams at much faster a rate than we used to.

sentences.en: Even in our field, while the rate of code output has increased substantially, the rate of value generation increase has been quite a bit more modest.
sentences.zh: 即使在我们的领域，虽然代码输出率大幅提高，但价值生成的增长率却相当温和。

sentences.en: I have observed, and heard from a number of other places, that while my own output has increased somewhat we still generally can't plan on being able to work with other teams at much faster a rate than we used to.
sentences.zh: 我观察到，并且从其他一些地方听说，虽然我自己的产出有所增加，但我们通常仍然无法计划能够以比以前更快的速度与其他团队合作。

blocks.html_en: There's a viable business here but I can't see how all these companies expect to be returning all this revenue in any financially sensible period of time. They're all spending like if only they spend enough they can own about %900 of the market in three years. They can't all do that, even accounting for "AI makes the market bigger".

sentences.en: There's a viable business here but I can't see how all these companies expect to be returning all this revenue in any financially sensible period of time.
sentences.zh: 这里有一个可行的业务，但我看不出所有这些公司如何期望在任何财务上合理的时间内返还所有这些收入。

sentences.en: They're all spending like if only they spend enough they can own about %900 of the market in three years.
sentences.zh: 他们都在花钱，好像只要他们花得足够多，他们就能在三年内拥有大约 900% 的市场份额。

sentences.en: They can't all do that, even accounting for "AI makes the market bigger".
sentences.zh: 即使考虑到“人工智能让市场变得更大”，他们也不能全部做到这一点。

blocks.html_en: And they're wildly vulnerable to some new solution coming out that obsoletes all this spending, like an ASIC that starts running a popular model directly (especially if model capabilities plateau), meaning that all this nVidia GPU spending is so much dead silicon. Or someone comes out with a much more efficient way to train models. There has to be some insight we're missing; humans do not learn what they do by having the entire contents of the Internet poured through their head hundreds of times over. We are far more efficient with our training data. What if someone works out a solution to that and we don't need to spend billions on GPUs but only millions? The whole spending proposition could collapse overnight and the companies that suddenly have three orders of magnitude too much hardware and the debt to match would be up a creek without a paddle.

sentences.en: And they're wildly vulnerable to some new solution coming out that obsoletes all this spending, like an ASIC that starts running a popular model directly (especially if model capabilities plateau), meaning that all this nVidia GPU spending is so much dead silicon.
sentences.zh: 而且他们非常容易受到一些新解决方案的影响，这些解决方案会废弃所有这些支出，例如直接开始运行流行模型的 ASIC（特别是如果模型功能处于稳定状态），这意味着所有这些 nVidia GPU 支出都是死硅。

sentences.en: Or someone comes out with a much more efficient way to train models.
sentences.zh: 或者有人提出了一种更有效的方法来训练模型。

sentences.en: There has to be some insight we're missing; humans do not learn what they do by having the entire contents of the Internet poured through their head hundreds of times over.
sentences.zh: 我们一定缺少一些洞察力；人类不会通过将互联网的全部内容在头脑中翻阅数百次来了解自己在做什么。

sentences.en: We are far more efficient with our training data.
sentences.zh: 我们使用训练数据的效率要高得多。

sentences.en: What if someone works out a solution to that and we don't need to spend billions on GPUs but only millions?
sentences.zh: 如果有人找到了解决方案，而我们不需要在 GPU 上花费数十亿美元，而只需花费数百万美元，该怎么办？

sentences.en: The whole spending proposition could collapse overnight and the companies that suddenly have three orders of magnitude too much hardware and the debt to match would be up a creek without a paddle.
sentences.zh: 整个支出计划可能会在一夜之间崩溃，那些突然拥有多出三个数量级硬件和与之相匹配的债务的公司将陷入无桨的小溪。

blocks.html_en: [1]: https://jerf.org/iri/post/2026/programming_is_engineering/

sentences.en: [1]: https://jerf.org/iri/post/2026/programming_is_engineering/

blocks.html_en: I very much agree with this. Even the top tier models today, without the unit tests, without integration tests, and domain experts reviewing the code would flounder for 50% of the work they do. Sure they can write the unit tests and integration tests themselves, but at that point you aren't in need of a specific system being built, but rather an out of the box solution would probably fit your needs. It does speed up the grunt boilerplate work of development quite a bit, it does help with gnarly bugs and the like, but expertise is still needed. And we as engineers/programmers have systems in place that make using AI easier, we have the human context windows to be able to parse the technical jargon the AI spits out. Will AI for the masses be akin to slightly better automation?

sentences.en: I very much agree with this.
sentences.zh: 我非常同意这一点。

sentences.en: Even the top tier models today, without the unit tests, without integration tests, and domain experts reviewing the code would flounder for 50% of the work they do.
sentences.zh: 即使是当今的顶级模型，如果没有单元测试、集成测试以及审查代码的领域专家，他们所做的 50% 的工作也会陷入困境。

sentences.en: Sure they can write the unit tests and integration tests themselves, but at that point you aren't in need of a specific system being built, but rather an out of the box solution would probably fit your needs.
sentences.zh: 当然，他们可以自己编写单元测试和集成测试，但此时您不需要构建特定的系统，而是开箱即用的解决方案可能会满足您的需求。

sentences.en: It does speed up the grunt boilerplate work of development quite a bit, it does help with gnarly bugs and the like, but expertise is still needed.
sentences.zh: 它确实大大加快了开发的繁重样板工作，它确实有助于解决棘手的错误等，但仍然需要专业知识。

sentences.en: And we as engineers/programmers have systems in place that make using AI easier, we have the human context windows to be able to parse the technical jargon the AI spits out.
sentences.zh: 作为工程师/程序员，我们拥有可以更轻松地使用人工智能的系统，我们拥有人类上下文窗口，能够解析人工智能吐出的技术术语。

sentences.en: Will AI for the masses be akin to slightly better automation?
sentences.zh: 面向大众的人工智能会类似于稍微更好的自动化吗？

blocks.html_en:
> we are the field getting the most out of AI, and it's not even close.

Just emphasizing that as, due to spending far too much time online the past week, I've been seeing a fair bit of this. "AI is definitely gaining popularity because all the software companies I know are going all in on it."

sentences.en: > we are the field getting the most out of AI, and it's not even close.Just emphasizing that as, due to spending far too much time online the past week, I've been seeing a fair bit of this.
sentences.zh: > 我们是从人工智能中获得最大收益的领域，但距离还很遥远。只是强调一下，由于过去一周在网上花费了太多时间，我已经看到了相当多的这种情况。

sentences.en: "AI is definitely gaining popularity because all the software companies I know are going all in on it."
sentences.zh: “人工智能确实越来越受欢迎，因为我认识的所有软件公司都在全力投入。”

blocks.html_en: For certain values of ‘dramatic improvement’. Is lots more important work being done with LLMs? Not much sign of it yet, they’ve been helpful for experts at times (e.g. vuln research or maths research) but that hardly justifies the vast sums for Google investors.

sentences.en: For certain values of ‘dramatic improvement’.
sentences.zh: 对于某些“显着改善”的价值观。

sentences.en: Is lots more important work being done with LLMs?
sentences.zh: 法学硕士是否正在做很多更重要的工作？

sentences.en: Not much sign of it yet, they’ve been helpful for experts at times (e.g. vuln research or maths research) but that hardly justifies the vast sums for Google investors.
sentences.zh: 目前还没有太多迹象，它们有时对专家很有帮助（例如漏洞研究或数学研究），但这很难证明谷歌投资者的巨额资金是合理的。

blocks.html_en:
The infamous 2025 MIT study that found almost all AI pilots in companies were failing, also found that virtually every worker was using AI many times a week if not daily.

Turns out people just use their personal AI accounts rather than company ones. Which would make sense if you want to claim the work the AI does as your own.

sentences.en: The infamous 2025 MIT study that found almost all AI pilots in companies were failing, also found that virtually every worker was using AI many times a week if not daily.Turns out people just use their personal AI accounts rather than company ones.
sentences.zh: 臭名昭著的 2025 麻省理工学院研究发现，几乎所有公司的人工智能试点都失败了，还发现几乎每个员工每周都会多次使用人工智能，甚至每天都会使用人工智能。事实证明，人们只是使用个人人工智能帐户，而不是公司帐户。

sentences.en: Which would make sense if you want to claim the work the AI does as your own.
sentences.zh: 如果你想将人工智能所做的工作归为你自己的，那么这是有道理的。

blocks.html_en:
>Presumably at some point you need a measurable productivity return yea?

At what point? This technology is brand new. Did you think we were going to double productivity in 3 years?

Capacity is being built. It's hard to build data centres, there are no chips, there is no memory, it's hard to get talent, we don't have the energy to power the facilities.

No one knows where this is going. We are scratching the surface. There is an absolute boom happening, and yet every day I have log onto Hacker News and read this nonsense about everything falling apart. Are we living in the same universe??? So-called "technologists" saying, "meh, it's not that cool". Okay.

Guess what? You're not Michael Burry. Nobody cares or will care that you "called it". Look around this place: you aren't even slightly contrarian.

sentences.en: >Presumably at some point you need a measurable productivity return yea?At what point?
sentences.zh: >大概在某个时候您需要可衡量的生产力回报是吗？在什么时候？

sentences.en: This technology is brand new.
sentences.zh: 这项技术是全新的。

sentences.en: Did you think we were going to double productivity in 3 years?Capacity is being built.
sentences.zh: 您认为我们会在 3 年内将生产力提高一倍吗？产能正在建设中。

sentences.en: It's hard to build data centres, there are no chips, there is no memory, it's hard to get talent, we don't have the energy to power the facilities.No one knows where this is going.
sentences.zh: 建设数据中心很困难，没有芯片，没有内存，很难获得人才，我们没有能源为设施提供动力。没有人知道这将走向何方。

sentences.en: We are scratching the surface.
sentences.zh: 我们正在触及表面。

sentences.en: There is an absolute boom happening, and yet every day I have log onto Hacker News and read this nonsense about everything falling apart.
sentences.zh: 绝对的繁荣正在发生，但我每天都会登录黑客新闻并阅读有关一切崩溃的废话。

sentences.en: Are we living in the same universe???
sentences.zh: 我们生活在同一个宇宙吗？？？

sentences.en: So-called "technologists" saying, "meh, it's not that cool".
sentences.zh: 所谓的“技术专家”说，“呃，这没那么酷”。

sentences.en: Okay.Guess what?
sentences.zh: 好吧，你猜怎么着？

sentences.en: You're not Michael Burry.
sentences.zh: 你不是迈克尔·伯里。

sentences.en: Nobody cares or will care that you "called it".
sentences.zh: 没有人关心或不会关心你“叫它”。

sentences.en: Look around this place: you aren't even slightly contrarian.
sentences.zh: 环顾四周：你连一点点逆反心理都没有。

blocks.html_en:
"We would be profitable if we had the resources but we don't," isn't the smackdown argument you seem to believe it is.

There used to be a thing where successful tech companies were profitable right out of the gate, and very successful companies doubled those profits for years, and companies who bought and used the tech could point to clear, actioned, benefits and cost savings.

Now it's all "This will be really, really profitable one day, probably, if the omens align and we can deal with all of the problems."

sentences.en: "We would be profitable if we had the resources but we don't," isn't the smackdown argument you seem to believe it is.There used to be a thing where successful tech companies were profitable right out of the gate, and very successful companies doubled those profits for years, and companies who bought and used the tech could point to clear, actioned, benefits and cost savings.Now it's all "This will be really, really profitable one day, probably, if the omens align and we can deal with all of the problems."
sentences.zh: “如果我们拥有资源，但我们没有资源，我们就会盈利”，这并不是你所相信的令人震惊的论点。过去有一种情况，成功的科技公司一开始就盈利，而且非常成功的公司多年来利润翻了一番，而购买和使用该技术的公司可能会指出明确的、行动的、好处和成本节约。现在这一切都是“如果预兆一致并且我们能够解决所有问题的话，也许有一天，这将是真正非常有利可图的。”

blocks.html_en:
It's an internet/railroad issue again.

Tech is real, impact is gigantic, long term winners hard to predict, capex spending hard to recoup soon, if ever.

And differently than internet or rails, you don't build once and maintain later, but enter a loop of ever increased spending to keep on top of the arms race and ever exploding usage.

sentences.en: It's an internet/railroad issue again.Tech is real, impact is gigantic, long term winners hard to predict, capex spending hard to recoup soon, if ever.And differently than internet or rails, you don't build once and maintain later, but enter a loop of ever increased spending to keep on top of the arms race and ever exploding usage.
sentences.zh: 这又是一个互联网/铁路问题。技术是真实的，影响是巨大的，长期赢家很难预测，资本支出很难很快收回（如果有的话）。与互联网或铁路不同的是，你不会构建一次并稍后维护，而是进入一个不断增加支出的循环，以保持军备竞赛和不断爆炸性的使用。

> not sure how to square this with the dramatic improvement in LLM capabilities

A good tech demo doesn’t matter to the business if the products don’t become profitable at the scale the investment chased.

blocks.html_en:
I'm not sure I've seen what I would call dramatic improvement since maybe GPT4?

Sure, things got better. But I'd call it iterative more than revolutionary. I still wouldn't trust any of the models to do anything meaningful unattended. They all still do dumb shit all the time.

Plus, even if they were genuinely dramatically better, the businesses sure as hell aren't. They're burning money left and right, they have no moat, Chinese open models are basically equivalent these days. What's the path to profitability, or hell, break-even? How do you envision this being anything but a giant money pit?

sentences.zh: 我仍然不相信任何模型能够在无人看管的情况下做任何有意义的事情。

> I'm not sure I've seen what I would call dramatic improvement since maybe GPT4?

LLM conversations online are so weird. Whenever I read things like this it’s like I’m living in a different world than the other person.

GPT4 was almost useless compared to what we have available today.

blocks.html_en:
I mostly use anthropic models, but there was a big step function when claude code came out, and it’s been incremental or a plateau since then.

Opus 4.6 and 4.8 are basically indistinguishable from Fable and Sonnet 5. 4.7 was a hot mess. The guardrails on 4.8 and 5.0 make them worse than 4.6 for many tasks. So, even if Fable is theoretically better, refusals/downgrades make it a worse product in practice. Who cares if it outperforms on 1-2% of real world tasks if 5-10% of tasks are blocked?

I’d bet most people could be downgraded to a 12 month old frontier model, and not notice for a week or so.

Anthropic’s big problem is that open weight models are 0-6 months behind. So, their product is commoditized and margins are never going to be good.

sentences.en: I mostly use anthropic models, but there was a big step function when claude code came out, and it’s been incremental or a plateau since then.Opus 4.6 and 4.8 are basically indistinguishable from Fable and Sonnet 5.
sentences.zh: 我主要使用人择模型，但克劳德代码出来时有一个很大的阶跃函数，此后一直是增量或平台期。Opus 4.6和4.8与Fable和Sonnet 5基本上没有区别。

sentences.en: 4.7 was a hot mess.
sentences.zh: 4.7 真是一团糟。

sentences.en: The guardrails on 4.8 and 5.0 make them worse than 4.6 for many tasks.
sentences.zh: 4.8 和 5.0 的护栏使它们在许多任务上比 4.6 更差。

sentences.en: So, even if Fable is theoretically better, refusals/downgrades make it a worse product in practice.
sentences.zh: 因此，即使《神鬼寓言》在理论上更好，拒绝/降级也会使其在实践中成为更糟糕的产品。

sentences.en: Who cares if it outperforms on 1-2% of real world tasks if 5-10% of tasks are blocked?I’d bet most people could be downgraded to a 12 month old frontier model, and not notice for a week or so.Anthropic’s big problem is that open weight models are 0-6 months behind.
sentences.zh: 如果 5-10% 的任务被阻塞，谁会在乎它在 1-2% 的现实世界任务上是否表现出色？我敢打赌，大多数人可能会被降级为 12 个月前的前沿模型，并且一周左右不会注意到。Anthropic 的大问题是开放权重模型落后 0-6 个月。

sentences.en: So, their product is commoditized and margins are never going to be good.
sentences.zh: 因此，他们的产品已经商品化，利润永远不会好。

blocks.html_en:
It sure is funny how everyone claims the current model is a "dramatic improvement" over the models from X months ago.

You'd think if there had been that many dramatic improvements I'd have to babysit an LLM less frequently.

blocks.html_en:
Because there are almost no "We used AI to save money, improve our services, and gain more customers" success stories.

There's a lot of "We fired a lot of people because we're sheep and now we're having to hire some of them back" stories. And a lot of "A few engineers are doing a lot more, but we're not quite sure how to turn that into actual money" stories. And even more "We told everyone to tokenmaxx, and they did, and then we realised it was costing too much, so we stopped," stories.

But there really hasn't been a deluge of "AI has cut costs and increased profits while also improving quality" stories.

There has been a small outbreak of vibe-startups offering fairly generic services - mostly marketing and adjacent - who are doing okay, possibly.

But established tech? Doubt.

sentences.en: Because there are almost no "We used AI to save money, improve our services, and gain more customers" success stories.There's a lot of "We fired a lot of people because we're sheep and now we're having to hire some of them back" stories.
sentences.zh: 因为几乎没有“我们利用人工智能来省钱、改善服务、赢得更多客户”的成功故事。有很多“我们解雇了很多人，因为我们是绵羊，现在我们不得不重新雇用其中一些人”的故事。

sentences.en: And a lot of "A few engineers are doing a lot more, but we're not quite sure how to turn that into actual money" stories.
sentences.zh: 还有很多“一些工程师做了更多的事情，但我们不太确定如何将其转化为实际的金钱”的故事。

sentences.en: And even more "We told everyone to tokenmaxx, and they did, and then we realised it was costing too much, so we stopped," stories.But there really hasn't been a deluge of "AI has cut costs and increased profits while also improving quality" stories.There has been a small outbreak of vibe-startups offering fairly generic services - mostly marketing and adjacent - who are doing okay, possibly.But established tech?
sentences.zh: 甚至还有更多“我们告诉每个人tokenmaxx，他们也这么做了，然后我们意识到成本太高，所以我们停止了”的故事。但实际上并没有大量的“人工智能降低了成本，增加了利润，同时也提高了质量”的故事。已经出现了一些提供相当通用服务的振动初创公司——主要是营销和邻近服务——他们可能做得还不错。但是成熟的技术呢？

sentences.en: Doubt.
sentences.zh: 怀疑。

blocks.html_en:
What would be the best thing to do with ones investments considering these alarms?

Say you had some money in cash rn, what should one do? Wait for a crash and buy stuff up cheap? Put it in some safe category?

This stuff is stressing me out and I do believe it's gonna come crashing down sooner or later, but I don't know enough about investments to know how to best come out unscathed.

sentences.zh: 考虑到这些警报，投资的最佳做法是什么？假设您有一些现金，应该做什么？

sentences.en: Wait for a crash and buy stuff up cheap?
sentences.zh: 等股市崩盘再买便宜货？

sentences.en: Put it in some safe category?This stuff is stressing me out and I do believe it's gonna come crashing down sooner or later, but I don't know enough about investments to know how to best come out unscathed.
sentences.zh: 把它放在某个安全的类别中？这些东西让我压力很大，我相信它迟早会崩溃，但我对投资了解不够，不知道如何最好地毫发无伤。

blocks.html_en:
Diversify! Historically, the average length of a recession has been 12-24 months. So set up a system whereby you won’t screw’s yourself over by selling when things are low, but instead you can weather the storm.

Build a rainy day fund. Determine how much cash you will need if you are out of a job and how long you think that will last, allocate some portion of that amount into low risk bonds. Russ way if you need cash you aren’t selling investments at a big loss.

If you have enough liquidity put some in real estate as a forced savings vehicle as it’s harder to liquidate than stocks. Then just sit out any coming storm.

sentences.en: Diversify!
sentences.zh: 多样化！

sentences.en: Historically, the average length of a recession has been 12-24 months.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，经济衰退的平均持续时间为 12-24 个月。

sentences.en: So set up a system whereby you won’t screw’s yourself over by selling when things are low, but instead you can weather the storm.Build a rainy day fund.
sentences.zh: 因此，建立一个系统，让你不会在价格低迷时抛售而搞砸自己，而是可以渡过难关。建立一支未雨绸缪基金。

sentences.en: Determine how much cash you will need if you are out of a job and how long you think that will last, allocate some portion of that amount into low risk bonds.
sentences.zh: 确定如果您失业，您将需要多少现金以及您认为这种情况会持续多久，然后将该金额的一部分分配给低风险债券。

sentences.en: Russ way if you need cash you aren’t selling investments at a big loss.If you have enough liquidity put some in real estate as a forced savings vehicle as it’s harder to liquidate than stocks.
sentences.zh: 拉斯的方式是，如果你需要现金，你就不会以巨大的损失出售投资。如果你有足够的流动性，请将一些资金投入房地产作为强制储蓄工具，因为它比股票更难变现。

sentences.en: Then just sit out any coming storm.
sentences.zh: 然后就静静地等待任何即将到来的风暴。

blocks.html_en: Build a rainy day fund. Determine how much cash you will need if you are out of a job and how long you think that will last, allocate some portion of that amount into low risk bonds. Russ way if you need cash you aren’t selling investments at a big loss.

sentences.en: Build a rainy day fund.
sentences.zh: 建立应急基金。

sentences.en: Determine how much cash you will need if you are out of a job and how long you think that will last, allocate some portion of that amount into low risk bonds.
sentences.zh: 确定如果您失业，您将需要多少现金以及您认为这种情况会持续多久，然后将该金额的一部分分配给低风险债券。

sentences.en: Russ way if you need cash you aren’t selling investments at a big loss.
sentences.zh: 拉斯的方式是，如果你需要现金，你就不会以巨大的损失出售投资。

blocks.html_en: If you have enough liquidity put some in real estate as a forced savings vehicle as it’s harder to liquidate than stocks. Then just sit out any coming storm.

sentences.en: If you have enough liquidity put some in real estate as a forced savings vehicle as it’s harder to liquidate than stocks.
sentences.zh: 如果您有足够的流动性，请将一些流动性投入房地产作为强制储蓄工具，因为它比股票更难变现。

sentences.en: Then just sit out any coming storm.
sentences.zh: 然后就静静地等待任何即将到来的风暴。

blocks.html_en:
But diversify into what?

If we assume this takes down the US economy and bonds, what then? International bonds/stocks? Won't those also be too entangled? Precious metals?

sentences.en: But diversify into what?If we assume this takes down the US economy and bonds, what then?
sentences.zh: 但多元化到什么？如果我们假设这会拖垮美国经济和债券，那么会怎样呢？

sentences.en: International bonds/stocks?
sentences.zh: 国际债券/股票？

sentences.en: Won't those also be too entangled?
sentences.zh: 那些会不会也太纠结了？

sentences.en: Precious metals?
sentences.zh: 贵金属？

blocks.html_en:
If there's a big AI bust, there will be no escaping it. Like 2008, the entire economy will slow down. This time it might even end in a war. A well diversified portfolio e.g. index funds will weather the storm and recover.

Build your emergency fund first if you don't have one. 6-12 months of salary in cash or CDs. Then dollar-cost average into well diversified equities. Don't watch them day-to-day. You're concerned about their value in 20-30 years, not tomorrow.

sentences.en: If there's a big AI bust, there will be no escaping it.
sentences.zh: 如果人工智能出现大萧条，那将是不可避免的。

sentences.en: Like 2008, the entire economy will slow down.
sentences.zh: 就像2008年一样，整个经济将会放缓。

sentences.en: This time it might even end in a war.
sentences.zh: 这一次甚至可能以战争告终。

sentences.en: A well diversified portfolio e.g. index funds will weather the storm and recover.Build your emergency fund first if you don't have one.
sentences.zh: 多元化的投资组合，例如指数基金将渡过难关并恢复元气。如果您没有应急基金，请先建立应急基金。

sentences.en: 6-12 months of salary in cash or CDs.
sentences.zh: 6-12个月的现金或CD工资。

sentences.en: Then dollar-cost average into well diversified equities.
sentences.zh: 然后将美元成本平均线投资于多元化的股票。

sentences.en: Don't watch them day-to-day.
sentences.zh: 不要每天盯着他们看。

sentences.en: You're concerned about their value in 20-30 years, not tomorrow.
sentences.zh: 你关心的是它们 20-30 年后的价值，而不是明天。

blocks.html_en:
Nobody can tell you what to diversify into without information about what you are concentrated in.

Sure, spread investments across stocks and bonds and treasuries from different markets.

But you can also diversify more broadly beyond economic capital to cultural and social capital. Learn new skills and build networks of generalized reciprocity with others before you (or they) need help.

sentences.en: Nobody can tell you what to diversify into without information about what you are concentrated in.Sure, spread investments across stocks and bonds and treasuries from different markets.But you can also diversify more broadly beyond economic capital to cultural and social capital.
sentences.zh: 如果不知道你所专注的领域，没有人能告诉你要进行多元化投资。当然，可以将投资分散到来自不同市场的股票、债券和国债。但你也可以更广泛地多元化，从经济资本到文化和社会资本。

sentences.en: Learn new skills and build networks of generalized reciprocity with others before you (or they) need help.
sentences.zh: 在您（或他们）需要帮助之前，学习新技能并与他人建立普遍互惠的网络。

blocks.html_en:
What problem? What alarms?

I see everyone around me doing way more work, of way more depth, than they ever did before using AI models. I see my company and friends of mine all paying large sums of money to Anthropic, Google, OpenAI to use AI models, and do more work than we did before.

So Google is investing in infrastructure which is HIGHLY in demand, there is much more demand than supply, and then they are making money from this infrastructure...

That's a good thing for Google, and as an investor in Google, I am glad they are making these investments.

sentences.en: What problem?
sentences.zh: 什么问题？

sentences.en: What alarms?I see everyone around me doing way more work, of way more depth, than they ever did before using AI models.
sentences.zh: 什么警报？我看到我周围的每个人都比他们使用人工智能模型之前所做的工作更多、更深入。

sentences.en: I see my company and friends of mine all paying large sums of money to Anthropic, Google, OpenAI to use AI models, and do more work than we did before.So Google is investing in infrastructure which is HIGHLY in demand, there is much more demand than supply, and then they are making money from this infrastructure...That's a good thing for Google, and as an investor in Google, I am glad they are making these investments.
sentences.zh: 我看到我的公司和我的朋友都向 Anthropic、Google、OpenAI 支付了大量资金来使用 AI 模型，并且比我们以前做更多的工作。所以 Google 正在投资需求量很大的基础设施，需求远大于供应，然后他们从这个基础设施中赚钱......这对 Google 来说是一件好事，作为 Google 的投资者，我很高兴他们进行这些投资。

blocks.html_en:
They raised their forecast a bit:

> The search giant now expects to spend between $195 billion and $205 billion in capital expenditures, its finance chief Anat Ashkenazi said on a conference call with analysts. The company said last quarter that it planned to spend between $180 billion and $190 billion this year.

sentences.en: They raised their forecast a bit:> The search giant now expects to spend between $195 billion and $205 billion in capital expenditures, its finance chief Anat Ashkenazi said on a conference call with analysts.
sentences.zh: 他们稍微提高了预测：> 该搜索巨头财务主管阿纳特·阿什肯纳齐 (Anat Ashkenazi) 在与分析师举行的电话会议上表示，目前预计资本支出将在 1,950 亿美元至 2,050 亿美元之间。

sentences.en: The company said last quarter that it planned to spend between $180 billion and $190 billion this year.
sentences.zh: 该公司上季度表示，今年计划支出 1800 亿至 1900 亿美元。

blocks.html_en:
Since when is investing in infrastructure burning money?

If there is a huge demand for shipping goods internationally, investing in ships and planes isn't burning money.

There is massive demand for compute in the world right now, Google is investing in that area. That's a good thing.

blocks.html_en:
> "If there is a huge demand for shipping goods internationally,
  >  investing in ships and planes isn't burning money.
  >  There is massive demand for compute in the world right now"

Emphasis on right now. CapEx makes sense if the demand is forecast to deliver enough profit over the expected lifespan of the investment to recoup the cost and margin.

There's enough hype and exuberance in the AI market that it's likely some players are going to be left holding the bag with a write-down on assets.

sentences.en: > "If there is a huge demand for shipping goods internationally, > investing in ships and planes isn't burning money. > There is massive demand for compute in the world right now" Emphasis on right now.
sentences.zh: > “如果对国际运输货物有巨大的需求， > 投资船舶和飞机并不是烧钱。 > 现在世界上对计算有巨大的需求” 强调现在。

sentences.zh: 如果预计需求能够在投资的预期生命周期内带来足够的利润以收回成本和利润，那么资本支出是有意义的。人工智能市场有足够的炒作和繁荣，很可能一些参与者会在资产减记的情况下承担责任。

blocks.html_en:
Serious investors look at balance sheets, less then what CEOs say. Elon Musk -- as an example-- says all kinds of things that don't really happen. Mark Zuckerberg is arguably less grandiose. When FB changed their name to Meta, said they were committed to the metaverse the stock didn't dump. When the really big investments in consumer VR hit Meta's balance sheet, there was a big drop.

Think of it as the difference between the waiter describing dishes with ingredients you don't really understand (or maybe even taste) vs presenting the bill for the meal.

sentences.en: Serious investors look at balance sheets, less then what CEOs say.
sentences.zh: 认真的投资者会关注资产负债表，而不是首席执行官所说的。

sentences.en: Elon Musk -- as an example-- says all kinds of things that don't really happen.
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）——举个例子——说了各种实际上并没有发生的事情。

sentences.en: Mark Zuckerberg is arguably less grandiose.
sentences.zh: 马克·扎克伯格可以说没有那么宏大。

sentences.en: When FB changed their name to Meta, said they were committed to the metaverse the stock didn't dump.
sentences.zh: 当 FB 更名为 Meta 时，表示他们致力于 Metaverse，但股票并未抛售。

sentences.en: When the really big investments in consumer VR hit Meta's balance sheet, there was a big drop.Think of it as the difference between the waiter describing dishes with ingredients you don't really understand (or maybe even taste) vs presenting the bill for the meal.
sentences.zh: 当对消费者 VR 的真正巨额投资冲击 Meta 的资产负债表时，出现了大幅下降。可以将其视为服务员用你不太了解（甚至可能品尝）的食材描述菜肴与呈现餐费账单之间的区别。

blocks.html_en:
If a company’s value was completely representated within their balance sheet, you would just run a computer program and be done. The problem is 1) balance sheets can be manipulated in legal ways to support a specific narrative 2) growth is governed by vision + strategy + execution.

For example, Apple the year before the iPhone got launched isn’t an attractive investment. They’re a one hit wonder with the iPod saving them from bankruptcy and the market has been fully saturated. The year the iPhone gets released their balanced sheet hasn’t really changed.

sentences.en: If a company’s value was completely representated within their balance sheet, you would just run a computer program and be done.
sentences.zh: 如果一家公司的价值完全体现在其资产负债表中，那么您只需运行一个计算机程序即可完成。

sentences.en: The problem is 1) balance sheets can be manipulated in legal ways to support a specific narrative 2) growth is governed by vision + strategy + execution.For example, Apple the year before the iPhone got launched isn’t an attractive investment.
sentences.zh: 问题是 1) 资产负债表可以通过合法方式操纵以支持特定的叙述 2) 增长由愿景 + 战略 + 执行控制。例如，iPhone 推出前一年的苹果公司并不是一项有吸引力的投资。

sentences.en: They’re a one hit wonder with the iPod saving them from bankruptcy and the market has been fully saturated.
sentences.zh: iPod 使他们免于破产，而且市场已经完全饱和，他们一炮而红。

sentences.en: The year the iPhone gets released their balanced sheet hasn’t really changed.
sentences.zh: iPhone发布的那一年，他们的资产负债表并没有真正改变。

blocks.html_en:
I've been seeing quite a few companies juicing short term margins and quarter to quarter maxxing even more than before, one such example:

https://x.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 https://xcancel.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 --- As someone who has personally spent $500k / mo+ on Google Ads for years, I can tell you with certainty:

This revenue growth in Search is artificial & extremely unhealthy for Google’s business long term

Search volumes are declining as legacy search is being increasingly cannibalized by non-monetized LLM queries

Google’s response?

Manufacture revenue growth via short-sighted, highly extractive, customer-hostile tactics. I.e. charge advertisers more for lower quality clicks, including clicks they do not want and explicitly did not approve Google to charge them for

A few examples to illustrate:

For all of its history until recently, Google operated on a 2nd price auction model

I.e. if you bid $5 CPC and the next highest bidder bids $1 CPC, Google charged you $1.01 for the click (one penny more than the 2nd highest bidder) rather than the $5 you bid

This was a genius move by Google early on as it incentivizes advertisers to input their true maximum willingness to pay rather than trying to play the game of bidding low and constantly adjusting to try to stay just ahead of the next highest bidder while still not paying too much

However recently, Google silently deprecated the 2nd price auction and began charging advertisers as much as their bid and budget caps allow, regardless of what anyone else is bidding

It’s a short-sighted cash grab at the expense of the long term health of the advertiser ecosystem

Making thing worse, Google also recently nerfed keyword targeting precision

Google previously had precise keyword targeting settings that allowed advertisers pick individual search phrases to bid on, defined down to the character w/ exact match or phrase match targeting

This was one of the core features that made search advertising magic, enabling advertisers to run extremely precise campaigns based on exactly what their target customer typed

But now, even if you bid on a specific term or phrase using the strictest exact -match targeting settings, Google will show your ad across 1000’s of unrelated keywords, labeling them as as “exact match (close variant)”

The definition of “close variant” means whatever they want it to and changes constantly. The result is advertisers get billed for clicks that are totally irrelevant to their business and that their targeting settings explicitly forbid Google from targeting. Google does it anyway and there’s no ability to turn this off

So now exact match is broad match, and broad match is just meaningless spam

This is all very bad for advertisers, but for Google, it allows them to show your ad and bill you for clicks across 1000x more searches that were previously going unmonetized (mainly because they’re garbage queries no one wants)

This is how you grow revenue atop declining search volumes

Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x. For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per day

And the extra spend is entirely on the garbage keywords Google arbitrarily throws in as “exact match (close variants)” which have no value to our business, but can’t be turned off

Google offers no refunds nor any recourse for overspend or spend on keywords you explicitly did not target

These are not the actions of a healthy business. These are the actions of company whose core business is in decline but desperately needs to pump quarterly earnings so Wall Street will continue to fund insane capex while hopefully looking through their rapidly deteriorating negative free cash flow

Google operated a benevolent monopoly for the better part of 25 yrs

Meaning the value Google captured from Search was but a small fraction of the value it created, and that spread produced a potential energy that justified expectations of high earnings growth far, far into the future

This is now no longer the case

At the alter of AI capex, Google is sacrificing the golden goose

sentences.en: if you bid $5 CPC and the next highest bidder bids $1 CPC, Google charged you $1.01 for the click (one penny more than the 2nd highest bidder) rather than the $5 you bidThis was a genius move by Google early on as it incentivizes advertisers to input their true maximum willingness to pay rather than trying to play the game of bidding low and constantly adjusting to try to stay just ahead of the next highest bidder while still not paying too muchHowever recently, Google silently deprecated the 2nd price auction and began charging advertisers as much as their bid and budget caps allow, regardless of what anyone else is biddingIt’s a short-sighted cash grab at the expense of the long term health of the advertiser ecosystemMaking thing worse, Google also recently nerfed keyword targeting precisionGoogle previously had precise keyword targeting settings that allowed advertisers pick individual search phrases to bid on, defined down to the character w/ exact match or phrase match targetingThis was one of the core features that made search advertising magic, enabling advertisers to run extremely precise campaigns based on exactly what their target customer typedBut now, even if you bid on a specific term or phrase using the strictest exact -match targeting settings, Google will show your ad across 1000’s of unrelated keywords, labeling them as as “exact match (close variant)”The definition of “close variant” means whatever they want it to and changes constantly.
sentences.zh: 如果您出价 5 美元，而次高出价者出价 1 美元，Google 会向您收取 1.01 美元的点击费用（比第二高出价者多出 1 便士），而不是您出价的 5 美元。这是 Google 早期的一个天才举措，因为它激励广告商输入他们真正的最大支付意愿，而不是试图玩低出价的游戏，并不断调整以试图保持领先于下一个最高出价者，同时仍然不支付太多。弃用第二次价格拍卖，并开始向广告商收取尽可能多的出价和预算上限，无论其他人出价多少这是一种短视的现金攫取，以广告商生态系统的长期健康为代价广告商可以根据目标客户输入的内容运行极其精确的广告活动。但是现在，即使您使用最严格的精确匹配定位设置对特定术语或短语进行出价，Google 也会在 1000 个不相关的关键字中展示您的广告，并将它们标记为“完全匹配（紧密变体）”。“紧密变体”的定义意味着他们想要的任何内容，并且不断变化。

sentences.en: The result is advertisers get billed for clicks that are totally irrelevant to their business and that their targeting settings explicitly forbid Google from targeting.
sentences.zh: 结果是广告商为与其业务完全无关的点击付费，并且他们的定位设置明确禁止 Google 进行定位。

sentences.en: Google does it anyway and there’s no ability to turn this offSo now exact match is broad match, and broad match is just meaningless spamThis is all very bad for advertisers, but for Google, it allows them to show your ad and bill you for clicks across 1000x more searches that were previously going unmonetized (mainly because they’re garbage queries no one wants)This is how you grow revenue atop declining search volumesLastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x.
sentences.zh: 谷歌无论如何都会这么做，而且没有能力关闭它所以现在精确匹配是广泛匹配，而广泛匹配只是毫无意义的垃圾邮件这对广告商来说都是非常糟糕的，但对于谷歌来说，它允许他们展示你的广告并为你收取以前未货币化的1000倍更多搜索的点击费用（主要是因为它们是没人想要的垃圾查询）这就是你在搜索量下降的情况下增加收入的最后，也许是最令人震惊的，谷歌悄悄地不再尊重预算上限为 2 倍。

sentences.en: For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per dayAnd the extra spend is entirely on the garbage keywords Google arbitrarily throws in as “exact match (close variants)” which have no value to our business, but can’t be turned offGoogle offers no refunds nor any recourse for overspend or spend on keywords you explicitly did not targetThese are not the actions of a healthy business.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们多年来一直在每日预算上限为 1000 美元的广告系列突然开始每天花费超过 2000 美元，而且额外的支出完全花在 Google 随意添加的“完全匹配（紧密变体）”垃圾关键字上，这些关键字对我们的业务没有任何价值，但无法关闭。Google 不会提供任何退款，也不会为超支或花费在您明确未定位的关键字上提供任何追索权。这些都不是健康企业的行为。

sentences.en: These are the actions of company whose core business is in decline but desperately needs to pump quarterly earnings so Wall Street will continue to fund insane capex while hopefully looking through their rapidly deteriorating negative free cash flowGoogle operated a benevolent monopoly for the better part of 25 yrsMeaning the value Google captured from Search was but a small fraction of the value it created, and that spread produced a potential energy that justified expectations of high earnings growth far, far into the futureThis is now no longer the caseAt the alter of AI capex, Google is sacrificing the golden goose
sentences.zh: 这些是核心业务正在下滑但迫切需要提高季度收益的公司的行为，因此华尔街将继续为疯狂的资本支出提供资金，同时希望能够审视其迅速恶化的负自由现金流谷歌在 25 年的大部分时间里一直处于仁慈的垄断地位这意味着谷歌从搜索中获取的价值只是其创造的价值的一小部分，而这种传播产生了一种潜在的能量，证明了对未来高收益增长的预期是合理的现在情况已不再是这样了人工智能资本支出中，谷歌正在牺牲金鹅

blocks.html_en:
I've been seeing quite a few companies juicing short term margins and quarter to quarter maxxing even more than before, one such example:

https://x.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 https://xcancel.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 --- As someone who has personally spent $500k / mo+ on Google Ads for years, I can tell you with certainty:

This revenue growth in Search is artificial & extremely unhealthy for Google’s business long term

Search volumes are declining as legacy search is being increasingly cannibalized by non-monetized LLM queries

Google’s response?

Manufacture revenue growth via short-sighted, highly extractive, customer-hostile tactics. I.e. charge advertisers more for lower quality clicks, including clicks they do not want and explicitly did not approve Google to charge them for

A few examples to illustrate:

For all of its history until recently, Google operated on a 2nd price auction model

I.e. if you bid $5 CPC and the next highest bidder bids $1 CPC, Google charged you $1.01 for the click (one penny more than the 2nd highest bidder) rather than the $5 you bid

This was a genius move by Google early on as it incentivizes advertisers to input their true maximum willingness to pay rather than trying to play the game of bidding low and constantly adjusting to try to stay just ahead of the next highest bidder while still not paying too much

However recently, Google silently deprecated the 2nd price auction and began charging advertisers as much as their bid and budget caps allow, regardless of what anyone else is bidding

It’s a short-sighted cash grab at the expense of the long term health of the advertiser ecosystem

Making thing worse, Google also recently nerfed keyword targeting precision

Google previously had precise keyword targeting settings that allowed advertisers pick individual search phrases to bid on, defined down to the character w/ exact match or phrase match targeting

This was one of the core features that made search advertising magic, enabling advertisers to run extremely precise campaigns based on exactly what their target customer typed

But now, even if you bid on a specific term or phrase using the strictest exact -match targeting settings, Google will show your ad across 1000’s of unrelated keywords, labeling them as as “exact match (close variant)”

The definition of “close variant” means whatever they want it to and changes constantly. The result is advertisers get billed for clicks that are totally irrelevant to their business and that their targeting settings explicitly forbid Google from targeting. Google does it anyway and there’s no ability to turn this off

So now exact match is broad match, and broad match is just meaningless spam

This is all very bad for advertisers, but for Google, it allows them to show your ad and bill you for clicks across 1000x more searches that were previously going unmonetized (mainly because they’re garbage queries no one wants)

This is how you grow revenue atop declining search volumes

Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x. For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per day

And the extra spend is entirely on the garbage keywords Google arbitrarily throws in as “exact match (close variants)” which have no value to our business, but can’t be turned off

Google offers no refunds nor any recourse for overspend or spend on keywords you explicitly did not target

These are not the actions of a healthy business. These are the actions of company whose core business is in decline but desperately needs to pump quarterly earnings so Wall Street will continue to fund insane capex while hopefully looking through their rapidly deteriorating negative free cash flow

Google operated a benevolent monopoly for the better part of 25 yrs

Meaning the value Google captured from Search was but a small fraction of the value it created, and that spread produced a potential energy that justified expectations of high earnings growth far, far into the future

This is now no longer the case

At the alter of AI capex, Google is sacrificing the golden goose

sentences.zh: 我看到很多公司都比以前更加追求短期利润和逐季度最大化，一个这样的例子：https://x.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 https://xcancel.com/MaxAnderson/status/2080229375773941871 --- 作为个人在 Google Ads 上花费了 50 万美元/月以上的人多年来，我可以肯定地告诉你：搜索领域的收入增长是人为的，对谷歌业务的长期发展极其不健康。随着传统搜索越来越多地被非货币化的法学硕士查询蚕食，搜索量正在下降。谷歌的反应是：通过短视、高度榨取、敌视客户的策略来制造收入增长。

blocks.html_en: So now exact match is broad match, and broad match is just meaningless spam

sentences.en: So now exact match is broad match, and broad match is just meaningless spam
sentences.zh: 所以现在精确匹配是广泛匹配，而广泛匹配只是毫无意义的垃圾邮件

blocks.html_en: This is all very bad for advertisers, but for Google, it allows them to show your ad and bill you for clicks across 1000x more searches that were previously going unmonetized (mainly because they’re garbage queries no one wants)

sentences.en: This is all very bad for advertisers, but for Google, it allows them to show your ad and bill you for clicks across 1000x more searches that were previously going unmonetized (mainly because they’re garbage queries no one wants)
sentences.zh: 这对广告商来说非常糟糕，但对谷歌来说，它允许他们展示你的广告，并向你收取以前无法盈利的 1000 倍搜索的点击次数（主要是因为它们是没人想要的垃圾查询）

blocks.html_en: This is how you grow revenue atop declining search volumes

sentences.en: This is how you grow revenue atop declining search volumes
sentences.zh: 这是在搜索量下降的情况下增加收入的方法

blocks.html_en: Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x. For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per day

sentences.en: Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x.
sentences.zh: 最后，或许也是最令人震惊的是，谷歌悄然停止遵守预算上限，其幅度是原来的两倍。

sentences.en: For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per day
sentences.zh: 例如，我们运行多年的每日预算上限为 1000 美元的营销活动突然开始每天支出超过 2000 美元

blocks.html_en: And the extra spend is entirely on the garbage keywords Google arbitrarily throws in as “exact match (close variants)” which have no value to our business, but can’t be turned off

sentences.en: And the extra spend is entirely on the garbage keywords Google arbitrarily throws in as “exact match (close variants)” which have no value to our business, but can’t be turned off
sentences.zh: 而额外的支出完全花在谷歌随意抛出的“完全匹配（紧密变体）”垃圾关键词上，这些关键词对我们的业务没有任何价值，但无法关闭

blocks.html_en: Google offers no refunds nor any recourse for overspend or spend on keywords you explicitly did not target

sentences.en: Google offers no refunds nor any recourse for overspend or spend on keywords you explicitly did not target
sentences.zh: 对于超支或花在您明确未定位的关键字上的费用，Google 不提供退款，也不提供任何追索权

blocks.html_en: These are not the actions of a healthy business. These are the actions of company whose core business is in decline but desperately needs to pump quarterly earnings so Wall Street will continue to fund insane capex while hopefully looking through their rapidly deteriorating negative free cash flow

sentences.en: These are not the actions of a healthy business.
sentences.zh: 这些都不是健康企业的行为。

sentences.en: These are the actions of company whose core business is in decline but desperately needs to pump quarterly earnings so Wall Street will continue to fund insane capex while hopefully looking through their rapidly deteriorating negative free cash flow
sentences.zh: 这些是核心业务正在下滑但迫切需要提高季度收益的公司的行为，因此华尔街将继续为疯狂的资本支出提供资金，同时希望能够摆脱其迅速恶化的负自由现金流

blocks.html_en: Google operated a benevolent monopoly for the better part of 25 yrs

sentences.en: Google operated a benevolent monopoly for the better part of 25 yrs
sentences.zh: 谷歌在 25 年的大部分时间里一直处于仁慈的垄断地位

blocks.html_en: Meaning the value Google captured from Search was but a small fraction of the value it created, and that spread produced a potential energy that justified expectations of high earnings growth far, far into the future

sentences.en: Meaning the value Google captured from Search was but a small fraction of the value it created, and that spread produced a potential energy that justified expectations of high earnings growth far, far into the future
sentences.zh: 这意味着谷歌从搜索中获取的价值只是其创造的价值的一小部分，而这种传播产生了一种潜在的能量，证明了对未来高收益增长的预期是合理的

blocks.html_en: This is now no longer the case

sentences.en: This is now no longer the case
sentences.zh: 现在情况已不再如此

blocks.html_en: At the alter of AI capex, Google is sacrificing the golden goose

sentences.en: At the alter of AI capex, Google is sacrificing the golden goose
sentences.zh: 在人工智能资本支出的变化中，谷歌正在牺牲金鹅

blocks.html_en:
> Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x. For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per day

When I worked on Google Ads (I left in 2020), I remember this one tripping a lot of people. As I remember it, the limit for a single day is indeed 2x daily budget, but over a month it will average to it. This is supposed to give more flexibility to the auto bidder.

sentences.en: > Lastly, and perhaps most egregiously, Google quietly stopped respecting budget caps by a factor of 2x.
sentences.zh: > 最后，也许是最令人震惊的是，谷歌悄然停止遵守预算上限，其幅度是原来的两倍。

sentences.en: For example campaigns we’ve been running for years with $1000 daily budget caps suddenly began spending $2000+ per dayWhen I worked on Google Ads (I left in 2020), I remember this one tripping a lot of people.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们多年来一直运行的每日预算上限为 1000 美元的广告系列突然开始每天花费超过 2000 美元。当我在 Google Ads 工作时（我于 2020 年离开），我记得这个让很多人绊倒。

sentences.en: As I remember it, the limit for a single day is indeed 2x daily budget, but over a month it will average to it.
sentences.zh: 我记得，单日的限制确实是每日预算​​的 2 倍，但超过一个月就会平均到这个限度。

sentences.en: This is supposed to give more flexibility to the auto bidder.
sentences.zh: 这应该为自动投标人提供更大的灵活性。

blocks.html_en: Basically all of Big Tech is betting it all on Red that this whole AI business pays off before they end up losing everything. And I get it, it would be unwise to stay behind and ignore what could very easily turn out to be humanity's greatest invention since pizza. But still, is there seriously no other way to go about it instead of collectively running head first, hands behind at a breakneck pace, while risking the complete collapse of ... well, everything? I suppose not, especially considering it's a technology with potentially massive military and social impact on a global scale, or even beyond that if we're being particularly delusional. Though one has to wonder who will end up paying the tab, and I think that we all know the answer to that.

sentences.en: Basically all of Big Tech is betting it all on Red that this whole AI business pays off before they end up losing everything.
sentences.zh: 基本上，所有大型科技公司都将全部赌注押在 Red 身上，认为整个人工智能业务在他们最终失去一切之前会得到回报。

sentences.en: And I get it, it would be unwise to stay behind and ignore what could very easily turn out to be humanity's greatest invention since pizza.
sentences.zh: 我明白了，落后并忽视这个很容易成为自披萨以来人类最伟大的发明是不明智的。

sentences.en: But still, is there seriously no other way to go about it instead of collectively running head first, hands behind at a breakneck pace, while risking the complete collapse of ...
sentences.zh: 但是，除了冒着彻底崩溃的风险集体以极快的速度前行、双手后退之外，真的没有其他办法可以解决这个问题吗？

sentences.en: well, everything?
sentences.zh: 好吧，一切？

sentences.en: I suppose not, especially considering it's a technology with potentially massive military and social impact on a global scale, or even beyond that if we're being particularly delusional.
sentences.zh: 我想不会，特别是考虑到这是一种在全球范围内可能产生巨大军事和社会影响的技术，如果我们特别有妄想的话，甚至会超出这一范围。

sentences.en: Though one has to wonder who will end up paying the tab, and I think that we all know the answer to that.
sentences.zh: 尽管人们不得不想知道谁最终会支付账单，但我认为我们都知道答案。

Are you clicking on more ads now or less. Are you using google search more now or less.

I'm using it a lot less.

Don't think Google can point to past revenue an indicator of future revenue, they need to establish new streams of revenue.

blocks.html_en: Sounds like Google is the only honest company in the market. Cash burn alone doesn’t mean much, it’s free cash flow vs return on invested capital that’s the real signal imo.

sentences.en: Sounds like Google is the only honest company in the market.
sentences.zh: 听起来谷歌是市场上唯一诚实的公司。

sentences.en: Cash burn alone doesn’t mean much, it’s free cash flow vs return on invested capital that’s the real signal imo.
sentences.zh: 现金消耗本身并没有多大意义，在我看来，自由现金流与投资资本回报率才是真正的信号。
